name: Brad Finstad
subject: The Left is fuming over President Trump’s endorsement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Finstad for Congress
body:
| | | | |
---
| Friend, When President Trump endorsed my campaign, I was deeply honored. But let’s be honest... the Left was furious. They know that when southern Minnesota stands with President Trump and me, their big-government, anti-farm agenda doesn’t stand a chance. I’m proud to work alongside President Trump to secure our border, stop reckless spending, defend our farmers, and protect our constitutional freedoms. But to keep this fight going, I need you on the team. Every dollar we raise right now helps us spread our conservative message, fight back against the liberal attacks, and win big in 2026. Can I count on your support today?
---
| | [Contribute $25, $50, $100 →](http://link.finstadforcongress.com/api/LinkHandler/getaction2?%3d)
---
| Together, we’ll keep fighting for Minnesota values and an America we can be proud of. – Brad

subject: Marcy Kaptur is on Frontline again

body:
The NRCC thinks they can flip OH-09

Peter,

Earlier this year, the DCCC announced its Frontline list for the 2026 midterms. Marcy Kaptur is on the list... again.

name: B. Shamsuddoha, NDRC
subject: 82 percent of Americans agree on this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Despite the clear will of the people, the GOP and Trump are recklessly redistricting wherever they can to keep their grip on power.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118810649/580971120/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk5ZjRjOGI1LTZjYmItZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGQ2ZjMzOCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Pq7kN6oOGBoVkxKrxtkP6NEF7fKHeG1e_e9gY5B4xBY=&emci=3ebb0f66-5ebb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&emdi=99f4c8b5-6cbb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, Political strategists often talk about “80-20” issues — the topics that the overwhelming majority of Americans agree upon, and that politicians can win on. Count gerrymandering as one of them. A new poll found that **82% of Americans agree** that redistricting should be done by independent commissions — not by the party in power. Even a large majority — 71% — of _Republican_ voters in GOP-controlled states agree that independent, non-partisan commissions should control district lines. Despite the clear will of the people, the GOP and Trump are recklessly redistricting wherever they can to keep their grip on power. We’re fiercely fighting back to ensure that this scheme backfires — and make clear that an extremist minority can’t take control of our democracy. [**The NDRC is the voice for the overwhelming majority of Americans who want to get redistricting out of the hands of partisan extremists. Donate today and help fight the extreme voices who are trying to exert their will over our democracy.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118810651/580971149/-1263916904?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_8020Issue_email_20251106-NDRC-Tier1-8020Issue_A&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251106-NDRC-Tier1-8020Issue_A&utm_campaign=8020Issue&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk5ZjRjOGI1LTZjYmItZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGQ2ZjMzOCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Pq7kN6oOGBoVkxKrxtkP6NEF7fKHeG1e_e9gY5B4xBY=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

In state after state, we’re seeing Republican politicians put Donald Trump’s demands over the needs of their own constituents.
But in state after state, Americans are rising up and telling gerrymandering politicians to let their voices be heard.
Just this week, a group of voters rallied against the Indiana governor’s attempt to redraw maps mid-decade — reflecting the majority of Indiana voters who reject this redistricting and stand for fairness.
Meanwhile, thousands of voters in Missouri protested Republican Governor Mike Kehoe’s special session to gerrymander Missouri’s maps, carrying signs that said “no rigged maps” and “let every vote count.” Citizens there are currently gathering signatures for a ballot initiative to ensure the voters have a chance to reject their blatant power grab.
This redistricting crisis has quickly gotten out of hand, thanks to a president who has no intention of being accountable to the people. [**We can defeat their efforts to undermine democracy, but we need to have the resources to fight multiple fires at once — make a donation today and let’s show that a loud and vocal majority of Americans oppose this GOP map rigging.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118810664/580971190/-1263916904?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_8020Issue_email_20251106-NDRC-Tier1-8020Issue_A&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251106-NDRC-Tier1-8020Issue_A&utm_campaign=8020Issue&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk5ZjRjOGI1LTZjYmItZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGQ2ZjMzOCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Pq7kN6oOGBoVkxKrxtkP6NEF7fKHeG1e_e9gY5B4xBY=)

clean_body: Despite the clear will of the people, the GOP and Trump are recklessly redistricting wherever they can to keep their grip on power. | Peter, Political strategists often talk about “80-20” issues — the topics that the overwhelming majority of Americans agree upon, and that politicians can win on. Count gerrymandering as one of them. A new poll found that 82% of Americans agree that redistricting should be done by independent commissions — not by the party in power. Even a large majority — 71% — of Republican voters in GOP-controlled states agree that independent, non-partisan commissions should control district lines. Despite the clear will of the people, the GOP and Trump are recklessly redistricting wherever they can to keep their grip on power. FEC_ decision completely changed the landscape of American politics -- it opened the door for billionaires, corporations, and special interests to spend unlimited, untraceable money to influence our elections.

With your support we can harness the power of our grassroots supporters to influence decision makers and put control back in the hands of the people.

name: Chris Ahuja
subject: The People are Speaking
body:
This is about who we refuse to become.

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=16c7a08e7b&e=5a6ee82cdb)
| | | | | |
Dear Peter,Tuesday’s results were a resounding **Hell No** to Donald Trump’s politics of hate and corruption. The Blue Wave that swept across America is an unmistakably clear message from communities across this country: We are done with politics that put billionaires before working families.The “anti-worker, pro-billionaire” agenda is not what America stands for. And voters are saying it loudly: **Enough**.On November 4th, we voted to push back on Donald Trump’s malicious, racist, misogynist administration.It was an historic day in America but those who are pushing cruel, unjust policies are not backing down. Trump and his lapdogs in the Republican Party are committed to cutting food assistance, attacking immigrant communities, denying workers a living wage, stripping away healthcare, and protecting corporate super-donors at all costs.So we say **Hell no**.We’re building a movement that protects workers, strengthens families, and centers community in every policy choice. **We say yes to:** **A government that cares about people**An economy that works for everyone**Communities that are safe, supported, and thrivingThis is our moment to push forward, together.Join us. Help us make things right and tell Donald Trump and the Republicans: **HELL NO**!This is about who we refuse to become.
---
| [Donate](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=a299460c51&e=5a6ee82cdb)

**Let’s go.
**[****](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=c29901aebb&e=5a6ee82cdb)**Chris Ahuja****Democrat for Congress, CA-32**
**No Fear. No Silence.**

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: I'm fighting for you in the Senate. Aaron will fight for you in Carson City.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada Rosen for Nevada
body:
Democrats are fighting back. We need Aaron with us!
| | |
---
Dear friend,
This week, Democrats won major victories in Virginia, New Jersey, California, and Pennsylvania. Now, we need to also elect a strong leader in Carson City who isn’t afraid to take on tough fights for Nevada.
**That’s why I’m proud to support Attorney General Aaron Ford for Governor of Nevada.[Will you please split a donation between Aaron Ford’s campaign for governor and my campaign today so we can keep fighting for you, our state, and our country?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118811315/580982143/661199874?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk5645-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZjI4YmQ3ZS02ZmJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NfRXCVFpxx4FITexsozBX0Ma4up2vjzKnsHmdRtoTOg=)**
Democratic victories across the country have made it clear that voters are **_done_** with the GOP making life harder for them.
**Americans don’t want Medicaid cuts to fund tax breaks for billionaires. They don’t want health care costs to skyrocket. And they don’t want SNAP food benefits to be cut for 42 million people.**
Unfortunately, after working overtime to pass Medicaid cuts earlier this year – Republicans in D.C. have done absolutely nothing all year to prevent a massive spike in health care costs. And now, they’re determined to keep doing nothing in Washington.
Last week, Senate Republicans blocked my resolution to provide emergency funding for SNAP benefits. Then, they overrode my attempt to keep the Senate working through the weekend to work on ending the shutdown. Instead, Senators went home for Halloween – and Trump went to Mar-a-Lago to hold a Great Gatsby-themed party.
The GOP has made it clear who they’re fighting for – Aaron Ford has, too.
As Nevada’s top law enforcement officer, Attorney General Ford has taken on giant corporations for jacking up prices and stood up to the Trump Administration time and again. He is someone who understands what hardworking families are going through right now. **As governor, I know he would continue his strong record of lowering prices, putting more money in Nevadans’ pockets, and keeping our communities safe.**
**I am thrilled by Democrats’ sweeping victories across the country last night. But now, we need your help to build on that momentum and secure major victories for Nevada in 2026: [Please, make a gift of $5 or more now – split between Aaron’s campaign and mine – to help Democrats continue to win major victories in Nevada and across the country, so we can fight for YOU!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118811316/580982144/661199874?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk5645-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZjI4YmQ3ZS02ZmJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NfRXCVFpxx4FITexsozBX0Ma4up2vjzKnsHmdRtoTOg=)**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Aaron Ford and Jacky Rosen will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $50](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118811317/580982145/661199874?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=jtk5645-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZjI4YmQ3ZS02ZmJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NfRXCVFpxx4FITexsozBX0Ma4up2vjzKnsHmdRtoTOg=)
---

| | [Donate another amount](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118811320/580982148/661199874?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk5645-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZjI4YmQ3ZS02ZmJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NfRXCVFpxx4FITexsozBX0Ma4up2vjzKnsHmdRtoTOg=)

Jacky

name: ElectJon.com
subject: Reaching out with an important request
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

**_Today is Thursday, November 6th_**
**We’re reaching out with an important request on behalf of Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign in Georgia.**
Good afternoon, Peter. We're asking you to reflect on the work Jon Ossoff does in the Senate to fight back against Trump and MAGA, and the importance of holding this seat if Democrats are going to have any chance at a majority.
It wasn't long ago that Democrats had control of the Senate, and it was Georgia voters who elected Jon Ossoff and Raphael Warnock to lock in the majority. **We can flip the Senate blue once again and stop MAGA, but Jon can't do it alone.**

**[If you agree that reelecting Jon Ossoff and taking back control of the Senate are goals worth fighting for, please give to Jon’s campaign today. There’s no contribution too small. Every dollar matters. Thank you.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-nov25.fw0_9v&refcode=em251106-1-subs-b&t=1&akid=1046%2E5691347%2EucYtci)

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Have any questions for me, Peter?
body:
What do you want to know?
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1898?t=1&akid=36%2E124072%2EooRXSs)
Peter, I got my start in politics running for office right where I grew up: Nash County. I wanted the folks in my home area of Nashville and Rocky Mount, NC and beyond to have the strongest possible advocate in their corner in Raleigh. I was able to win because of the _people_ who knew me, believed in me, and trusted me to do right by them. [](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/ask-roy/?t=6&akid=36%2E124072%2EooRXSs) Now, I’m running for the U.S. Senate — and a lot has changed from that first campaign. I also have experience running in tough, hard-fought statewide races. I’ve had the privilege of serving our state as Governor and Attorney General. **And while statewide campaigns don’t give me as many opportunities to meet with as many individual people as I did in that first election, getting to know folks personally — and earning their trust — is still at the heart of this campaign.** Peter, even if we don’t get to meet face to face, I want you to get to know me and to trust me to represent the American people in the U.S. Senate. So if you have any questions for me, I’d love to hear from you. And if you keep an eye on emails from our team, you’ll see my answers to a few select questions — maybe yours! **[If you click here, you can ask me a question or two, but they don’t only have to be about politics. Favorite BBQ joint, my thoughts on the ongoing Carolina Hurricanes season, that’s fair game too. I’d be glad to hear from you.](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/ask-roy/?t=7&akid=36%2E124072%2EooRXSs)** Looking forward to seeing your questions, Roy Cooper [](https://act.roycooper.com/survey/ask-roy/?t=8&akid=36%2E124072%2EooRXSs)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=2&akid=36%2E124072%2EooRXSs)** | [DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?t=3&akid=36%2E124072%2EooRXSs)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?t=4&akid=36%2E124072%2EooRXSs)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=5&akid=36%2E124072%2EooRXSs)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

clean_body: What do you want to know? | Peter, I got my start in politics running for office right where I grew up: Nash County. I wanted the folks in my home area of Nashville and Rocky Mount, NC and beyond to have the strongest possible advocate in their corner in Raleigh. I was able to win because of the people who knew me, believed in me, and trusted me to do right by them. Now, I’m running for the U.S. Senate — and a lot has changed from that first campaign. I also have experience running in tough, hard-fought statewide races. I’ve had the privilege of serving our state as Governor and Attorney General. And while statewide campaigns don’t give me as many opportunities to meet with as many individual people as I did in that first election, getting to know folks personally — and earning their trust — is still at the heart of this campaign. Peter, even if we don’t get to meet face to face, I want you to get to know me and to trust me to represent the American people in the U.S. Senate. So if you have any questions for me, I’d love to hear from you. And if you keep an eye on emails from our team, you’ll see my answers to a few select questions — maybe yours! Looking forward to seeing your questions, Roy Cooper Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here. National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us. Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party. Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy. ** | mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper Victory Fund PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal. Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. # [Amped Up: GOP Reps Say DOJ Has Trump/Epstein Pics *MUCH WORSE* Than Known](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=178197866&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MTk3ODY2LCJpYXQiOjE3NjI0Nzk0OTYsImV4cCI6MTc2NTA3MTQ5NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.wRT2c5OxEESKlnlyXHI5nEsagdlAkx9HwrxfCNgIDFw)
### Panic in the GOP, Truth in the Files, and Hope in the Fight for Democracy
| | [Cliff Schecter](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge), [David Shuster](https://substack.com/@davidshuster), [Dean Obeidallah](https://substack.com/@deanobeidallah), and [Blue Amp](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---
| Nov 7
---
| [](https://substack.com/@cliffsedge)| | [](https://substack.com/@davidshuster)| | [](https://substack.com/@deanobeidallah)| | [](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---|---|---|---|---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=178197866&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc4MTk3ODY2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MjQ3OTQ5NiwiZXhwIjoxNzY1MDcxNDk2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzcyMjUzMiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9UF0mX7RToymbC1n8rH4vXjMui8p9r6MNa47yQH-vQk&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/af2da0c4-3925-4855-9091-9c36c9814970?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/24ed41b8-7f69-4005-bd8d-3df2c72950ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
When David Shuster drops a story like this, it doesn't just make waves--it creates political tsunamis. This week on _Amped Up_ , Shuster broke open one of the most explosive rumors rocking Washington: Republican members of Congress are terrified about what's in the Epstein files--and what those files might reveal about Donald Trump.
Word from DOJ backchannels is that the evidence is "much worse than known," and that panic is now spreading through the GOP caucus faster than a Fox News lie.
Let's be clear: Shuster doesn't deal in gossip. He's one of the most meticulous reporters in the business, and when he says Republicans are scrambling to distance themselves from Trump, it's because the fear is real.
* * *
Join the movement and keep up to date for free.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.YtM5btTsHQ2aKaa6Z6DmWZZa4BZUd7lhc_aQ3lQmlyg?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=178197866)
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/dc3f45b9-643f-4642-bb97-0b426bfef9f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
We're talking about photos, videos, and data that could blow apart the last remnants of Trump's carefully staged myth. Lawmakers are now ready to vote to release the Epstein files the moment the discharge petition is signed--some just to save their own careers before the inevitable reckoning.
Enter my good friend and this week's guest, **Dean Obeidallah** --radio host, lawyer, comedian, and one of the smartest, funniest progressives in America. Dean brings the rare combination of intellect and heart that this movement needs. He reminded us that this is about more than schadenfreude--it's about justice, decency, and the soul of the country.
As Dean said, "Being a Democrat means caring about people I'll never meet." That's the moral line that separates us from the cruelty of Trumpism--the same cruelty starving kids and gutting SNAP benefits while billionaires build gold-plated ballrooms.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3f2c27cc-7f19-4ed1-ba93-ad2116accde9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dean also dug deep into the state of our politics--calling out Pam Bondi's years of protecting Epstein while pretending to champion victims, exposing how the GOP's cruelty isn't accidental but addictive.
And he brought optimism: the No Kings protests growing, voters turning out, and a new generation of Democrats rising on the language of affordability and justice. Dean's take? Keep fighting. Stay bold. Don't let the pundit class tell you that courage is bad politics.
We also talked about **Zoran Mamdani 's stunning victory** in New York City--a generational shift toward economic populism and compassion that terrifies the donor class. And yes, we talked about unity--two Jews and a Palestinian on one show, talking about hope, empathy, and fighting fascism together. That's America at its best.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5552ba77-8b97-4458-9eb0-ea431dd7fe00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
If you want to see what happens when progressives fight unapologetically for the working class, you're seeing it now. Democrats are winning because we're finally talking about what matters--affordability, fairness, and human dignity.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/24ed41b8-7f69-4005-bd8d-3df2c72950ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
And if the Epstein files do come out? Then maybe, just maybe, the last mask will fall off the face of Trumpism, and Americans will see exactly what rot has been festering under all that fake gold paint.
Before I wrap, if you missed some of our recent coverage, don't sleep on these:
👉 **"[Dems Crush GOP: Entering 2026, Eyes on WINNING 150 House Seats](https://substack.com/redirect/61f57fca-d6ff-4a32-9a73-8609133bb28f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"** -- our deep dive into why Democrats are surging.
👉 **"[Starving 40 Million Americans? Trump's Sick Sadism Knows No Bounds](https://substack.com/redirect/39cfbbd5-b56f-4b1c-8c99-0c457cefd38a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"** -- a brutal look at Trump's assault on working families.
👉 **"[Top Dem Insider EXPOSES Billionaires Controlling Our Politics](https://substack.com/redirect/2804c4fe-3e75-4905-a063-8ba12a8eed8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"** -- because sunlight is still the best disinfectant.
Those pieces set the stage for what's happening right now--the awakening of a movement that refuses to be silent, refuses to be small, and refuses to play by the fascists' rules.
* * *
Subscribe for more great interviews like this!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.YtM5btTsHQ2aKaa6Z6DmWZZa4BZUd7lhc_aQ3lQmlyg?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=178197866)
* * *
Dean closed the show with a reminder that stuck with me: "We might be the generation that saves democracy--or the one that loses it." The difference depends on what we do next. And he's right.
**The billionaires, the theocrats, and the wannabe kings are counting on our exhaustion.**
**Let 's prove them wrong.**
_**Because at Blue Amp Media, we don 't get tired. We get louder.**_
--Cliff
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/24ed41b8-7f69-4005-bd8d-3df2c72950ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

This week on Amped Up , Shuster broke open one of the most explosive rumors rocking Washington: Republican members of Congress are terrified about what's in the Epstein files--and what those files might reveal about Donald Trump. Word from DOJ backchannels is that the evidence is "much worse than known," and that panic is now spreading through the GOP caucus faster than a Fox News lie. Let's be clear: Shuster doesn't deal in gossip. He's one of the most meticulous reporters in the business, and when he says Republicans are scrambling to distance themselves from Trump, it's because the fear is real. Join the movement and keep up to date for free. | | ---|---|--- We're talking about photos, videos, and data that could blow apart the last remnants of Trump's carefully staged myth. Lawmakers are now ready to vote to release the Epstein files the moment the discharge petition is signed--some just to save their own careers before the inevitable reckoning. Enter my good friend and this week's guest, Dean Obeidallah --radio host, lawyer, comedian, and one of the smartest, funniest progressives in America. Dean brings the rare combination of intellect and heart that this movement needs. He reminded us that this is about more than schadenfreude--it's about justice, decency, and the soul of the country. As Dean said, "Being a Democrat means caring about people I'll never meet." That's the moral line that separates us from the cruelty of Trumpism--the same cruelty starving kids and gutting SNAP benefits while billionaires build gold-plated ballrooms. | | ---|---|--- Dean also dug deep into the state of our politics--calling out Pam Bondi's years of protecting Epstein while pretending to champion victims, exposing how the GOP's cruelty isn't accidental but addictive. And he brought optimism: the No Kings protests growing, voters turning out, and a new generation of Democrats rising on the language of affordability and justice. Dean's take? Keep fighting. Stay bold. Don't let the pundit class tell you that courage is bad politics. We also talked about Zoran Mamdani 's stunning victory in New York City--a generational shift toward economic populism and compassion that terrifies the donor class. And yes, we talked about unity--two Jews and a Palestinian on one show, talking about hope, empathy, and fighting fascism together. That's America at its best. | | ---|---|--- If you want to see what happens when progressives fight unapologetically for the working class, you're seeing it now. Democrats are winning because we're finally talking about what matters--affordability, fairness, and human dignity. | | ---|---|--- And if the Epstein files do come out? Before I wrap, if you missed some of our recent coverage, don't sleep on these: 👉 "" -- our deep dive into why Democrats are surging. 👉 "" -- a brutal look at Trump's assault on working families. 👉 "" -- because sunlight is still the best disinfectant. Subscribe for more great interviews like this! Dean closed the show with a reminder that stuck with me: "We might be the generation that saves democracy--or the one that loses it." The difference depends on what we do next. And he's right. The billionaires, the theocrats, and the wannabe kings are counting on our exhaustion. Let 's prove them wrong. body:

---
Derek —
We're pretty sure you didn't wake up this morning, jump out of bed and say, "Today is the day I am going to split a donation between Jon Ossoff's U.S. Senate campaign and John Fetterman’s!"
_(If you did, great! Here’s the link and thank you in advance:[secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jf-email-ossoff](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51050?refcode=jtk316-bu-fl&t=1&akid=10409%2E434096%2EDe4vX3))_
Otherwise, please give us a second to explain why we’re asking you to chip in and why particularly right now.
Jon won his last election by building the largest on-the-ground movement that Georgia had ever seen.
Doing it again — and going _even bigger_ — is not going to be easy. It is going to take sustained effort from now through Election Day. And we have to get to work right away.
Unfortunately, a lot of people think maybe it's too early to give. Or they'll wait until later. We get that.
But the truth is _early money to a campaign is more important than a donation at any other time._
Your donation now will help us build the strong foundation we need to win and ensure we’re ready for anything that comes our way on this campaign, from Donald Trump, our MAGA opponents, or anyone else. **So what do you say?**
**[Can Jon count on you to chip in $5, or any amount to his campaign and John Fetterman’s today? We’ve got a tough road ahead of us, and every dollar makes a difference.](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51050?refcode=jtk316-bu-fl&t=2&akid=10409%2E434096%2EDe4vX3)**

name: Gavin Newsom
subject: Crazy idea
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CAMPAIGN FOR DEMOCRACY PAC
body:
I'm not sending this message to millions of people.

**I'm writing with a bit of a crazy idea.**
I'm not sending this everyone on our email list. It's only going to a small number of folks because I want to try something a few people think is a bit crazy.
There are two things going on right now:
I have a book coming out in a few weeks. It's called "Young Man in a Hurry." It's a good book. Very personal. Not your normal political book at all. But of course, there will be a bunch of political stories you'll enjoy. I think you'd like it.
At the same time, we just spent a bunch of money on passing Prop 50, so now I need to refill that coffers at my Campaign for Democracy for the fights ahead -- including helping other states pass redistricting to stop Trump from rigging the next election.
So here's the part that may be a little crazy.
If you make a contribution of ANY AMOUNT, I'll send you a copy of my new book when it comes out. The average cost per book is $22.45 including shipping. If you can give that, great. If you can only give $5, great. If you want to cover the cost for a few folks who can only give a few bucks, great.
[**But make a contribution of ANY AMOUNT today and I will send you a copy of my new book, "Young Man in a Hurry." If we raise more than costs, every dollar will go toward helping Democrats fight back against Trump's effort to rig the next election.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ggn-book-any?refcode=em251106-1-b6&refcode2=2763_4767699_b4dneM&akid=2763%2E4767699%2Eb4dneM)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
subject: 22 million Americans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
"Waste, fraud, and abuse"
#
Republicans have kept the government shut down for 37 days because they think it’s “waste, fraud, and abuse” to give healthcare to more than 22 million Americans.
22 million Americans receive ACA subsidies because they don’t get insurance through their job, are too young for Medicare, and not poor enough for Medicaid.1
MAGA wants insurance premiums to double for 22 million Americans on January 1.
Why? Because **they’ve been given an assignment to rob Medicaid, Medicare, and Social Security to pay for tax cuts for billionaires**.
Now, they want to sign onto one, little, extra year of ACA subsidies.
Want to know why? Midterm elections are taking place next year.
They want us to fall for the same old fine-print, tricks, and politics around this.
**It is laughable**.
[But Alexandria and our movement are standing up across the country against these attacks on the working class. Will you chip in $5 today to power our fight to demand healthcare, a living wage, and so much more as basic rights?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251106.plgL4m&refcode=em20251106-10159-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10159%2E2526796%2EB5SGjK)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251106.plgL4m&refcode=em20251106-10159-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=10159%2E2526796%2EB5SGjK)**
Whether or not you can give today, thank you for standing with our movement.
In solidarity,
Team AOC

subject: Pedal to the metal

body:

Peter ,
It’s been a great week for Democrats, but we can’t let up!
New rankings from Roll Call just dropped, and they show exactly what we’re up against in 2026 since Republicans rewrote congressional maps across the country to protect their fragile House majority. They’ve already put some of our most vulnerable Democrats at the top of their target list!
From Don Davis in North Carolina to Vicente Gonzalez in Texas, the GOP’s new gerrymanders are designed to erase Democratic seats and silence voters in communities that stood up to Trump.
And that’s why we can’t afford to coast or get comfortable.
After a week of major victories, if we want to keep winning, we have to keep going.
We’re building the infrastructure to expand our reach and bring our game-winning turnout program to even more races in 2026, but it all depends on the resources we raise right now.
Will you chip in tonight to help us keep the momentum going and defend these vulnerable Democratic seats? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email11062025]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email11062025]
Let’s keep moving forward,
GOTV PAC

We’ll keep this short, Team: **The MAGA-ified U.S. Department of Justice has been demanding voter data from the states** , requesting that election officials turn over unredacted copies of voter rolls and every voter registration application submitted in the past 18 months. Several states with MAGA-aligned Secretaries of State and opponents of voting rights in charge of elections have complied, but other states are holding out to protect voters from intimidation, including Maine. **DOJ has escalated their attacks in Maine** , with suppressive former Trump lawyer, now Assistant Attorney General Harmeet Dhillon demanding an electronic copy of the voter registration list that “ _must contain all fields, including the registrant’s full name, date of birth, residential address, his or her state driver’s license number or the last four digits of the registrant’s social security number_ ” from December 1, 2023, through July 1, 2025.Folks, the U.S. Constitution entrusts the states, not the president or his agencies, to run elections. There's no legal basis for the DOJ to demand such sensitive and personally identifying information. **It's yet another overreach by a president fueled by lies and taken by sycophants who are more loyal to his every whim than the rule of law they swore an oath to uphold.** **[Team, voters in Maine and every other state deserve safe, secure, private elections, free of intimidation. name: Sarah McBride
subject: How we win
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
The vibes are good right now. Let’s keep them going.
#
Tuesday night was more than a win — it was proof that we show up, listen, and fight for each other, we win _everywhere._
[**If you’re ready to turn this week’s momentum into real progress, keep my voice in Congress, and build the big tent we need help flip the House in 2026, please consider chipping in $5 now.**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251106-1.InrPgq&refcode=em20251106-2477&amount=5&t=1&akid=2477%2E21716%2EufDyhk)
Democrats of every kind — from small towns to big cities, longtime organizers to newcomers, progressive to moderate — won this week.
What united these victories, despite the candidates' differences? They all _listened._ Each campaign met people where they are and built platforms on what their communities actually needed, not pundits’ talking points. They focused on lowering costs, making life easier for working families, and standing up to bullies — because that’s what people are desperate for right now.
**That’s what real leadership looks like:** candidates who reflect their communities. Our coalition’s diversity isn’t a weakness. It’s our biggest strength.
There’s been plenty of debate about what policies to emphasize, what groups to target, and what words to use (and not use) to win the midterms next year. But Tuesday night settled it:
If we want to build a party of inclusion — _and if we want to reject the politics of exclusion_ — then we have to make space for anyone who believes in an economy that works for everyone, protecting our democracy, and defending our values.
Because we don’t win by narrowing our coalition or targeting specific groups.
**We win by welcoming _everyone,_ no matter who they are, into our tent.**
So let’s keep these good vibes going. Let’s keep organizing, talking to each other, and finding common ground. Let’s keep building a movement where everyone has a place — and where we don’t just talk about progress, we deliver it.
[**If you believe in a politics that welcomes _all_ of us, then I hope you’ll chip in today to help us grow our movement. The 2026 election is one year away, and we need you with us.**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251106-1.InrPgq&refcode=em20251106-2477&amount=5&t=2&akid=2477%2E21716%2EufDyhk)

clean_body: The vibes are good right now. Let’s keep them going. Tuesday night was more than a win — it was proof that we show up, listen, and fight for each other, we win everywhere. Democrats of every kind — from small towns to big cities, longtime organizers to newcomers, progressive to moderate — won this week. What united these victories, despite the candidates' differences? They all listened. Each campaign met people where they are and built platforms on what their communities actually needed, not pundits’ talking points. They focused on lowering costs, making life easier for working families, and standing up to bullies — because that’s what people are desperate for right now. That’s what real leadership looks like: candidates who reflect their communities. Our coalition’s diversity isn’t a weakness. It’s our biggest strength. There’s been plenty of debate about what policies to emphasize, what groups to target, and what words to use (and not use) to win the midterms next year. But Tuesday night settled it: If we want to build a party of inclusion — and if we want to reject the politics of exclusion — then we have to make space for anyone who believes in an economy that works for everyone, protecting our democracy, and defending our values. Because we don’t win by narrowing our coalition or targeting specific groups. We win by welcoming everyone, no matter who they are, into our tent. So let’s keep these good vibes going. Let’s keep organizing, talking to each other, and finding common ground. Let’s keep building a movement where everyone has a place — and where we don’t just talk about progress, we deliver it. Let’s keep showing up for each other, and keep proving what’s possible when we do. Thank you for your support, — Sarah

name: David Hogg
subject: A miracle just happened — I shut Scott Jennings up
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
I feel kinda bad for doing Scott Jennings this dirty (actually, on second thought, no I don’t.)
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251106-1&t=1&akid=2814%2E587540%2ESIKs82)
Peter,
You might know Scott Jennings as everyone’s favorite professional lib rage-baiter, MAGA court jester, or fascist propagandist. But did you know he’s also apparently an _expert_ on affordability and what working people go through?
**When I was on CNN with him this week, and he started running his mouth about affordability, I was left with no choice but to roast him.**
[](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251106_1_rolex.vJiatR&refcode=em20251106-2814&amount=5&t=3&akid=2814%2E587540%2ESIKs82)
For reasons beyond me, CNN keeps paying Scott to play “working-class whisperer” as he sits in a DC studio, flashing his expensive watch to the camera, and pretending he knows what it’s like to worry about making ends meet.
So on the panel, when he started lecturing Zohran Mamdani about affordability, I’d had enough. I turned to Scott and said:
> _“Honestly, I think part of the reason why Zohran is so powerful is because**people are tired of being lectured by men with Rolexes on CNN about affordability in this country.”**_
And something amazing happened, Peter — for maybe the first time, Scott Jennings had _nothing_ to say.
The look on his face when he realized how bad he just got cooked? _Priceless._
Now, I’m not telling you this because I enjoy roasting pundits who make a living spewing lies and whatever flavor of hate MAGA’s selling that day (okay, maybe a little). It’s because this moment perfectly captures what’s broken in politics — and why Leaders We Deserve exists.
There are leaders like Zohran Mamdani who listen to their community, build policy around people’s needs, and fight for working families. Then there are people like Scott Jennings, who make six (probably more) figures kissing Trump’s butt while pretending to speak for the working class.
Democrats won big this week because of young, bold candidates who told voters the truth and ran on real plans to make life more affordable. That’s the new generation of leadership we’re building — powered by people, not pundits — and we need to build off this week’s momentum before it slips away.
If you want to see more grassroots candidates fight for working people (and more Rolex-wearing pundits get roasted), **[please chip in $5 today to help us elect the next wave of Democrats _who actually get it._](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251106_1_rolex.vJiatR&refcode=em20251106-2814&amount=5&t=4&akid=2814%2E587540%2ESIKs82)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251106_1_rolex.vJiatR&amount=5&refcode=em20251106-2814&t=23&akid=2814%2E587540%2ESIKs82)**
The future isn't being built in cable news studios — it’s being built by organizers, voters, and young leaders across the country who refuse to wait our turn. Like the hundreds I met at Zohran's victory party the next evening after that CNN interview.
Thank you for fueling this movement.
— David
P.S. Scott went back on CNN the next night and tried to say Tuesday’s election was terrible for Democrats.
Yeah, I’m sure sweeping every competitive race and landslide Democratic victories is _just awful._ [I tweeted at him](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/3868?t=24&akid=2814%2E587540%2ESIKs82) and asked if he had his Rolex on when he said that, too. Moments like this actually matter — we’re not just changing the message, but changing the messenger, and believe it or not, viral moments like this do make a difference and can bring more people into our movement.
Let’s keep going — and never let Scott Jennings forget how bad he got roasted. (He _really_ walked right into it — [here's the link to the clip.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/3869?t=25&akid=2814%2E587540%2ESIKs82)
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20251106-1&t=26&refcode2=2814_587540_SIKs82&akid=2814%2E587540%2ESIKs82)**

* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=27&akid=2814%2E587540%2ESIKs82)

subject: The tide is turning…

Democrats just won critical elections nationwide — showing Donald Trump and the entire Republican Party that _we are not done fighting._ [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025?refcode=em_20251106_5182_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5182_22930333_umekxi&akid=5182%2E22930333%2Eumekxi) Across the map, we put up strong candidates, ran strategic campaigns, and drove high Democratic turnout with a relentless focus on what matters most to people — affordability and kitchen table issues. name: Ron Wyden
subject: Republicans have created a health care nightmare
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118812859/581016050/2140832878?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOGQ0OTJlMC04NGJiLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRkNmYzMzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WY9COgej9ktptRtMKcTVxdPN7wm4xFdNvlN55sFritI=&emci=373824c3-91ba-f011-8e61-00224824e08d&emdi=d8d492e0-84bb-f011-8e61-6045bdd6f338&ceid=9818364)
---

| | This week, millions of Americans are logging on to their computers for open enrollment in the health care marketplace. They're going to sit down and try to choose the health care insurance plan that fits their needs, and the needs of their family. Except this year, things will look a little different. Because for the first time, MILLIONS of families will log on to see that next year, their insurance premiums are going to double, even triple. For months, Democrats have tried to negotiate with Republicans to stop these price increases from going into effect. We've been met with radio silence. In fact, Republicans are so committed to letting these price increases happen, that they shut down the entire federal government to make sure it happens. Millions of Americans rely on the Affordable Health Care exchange to get quality, affordable health care. For those families, these health care plans are nothing short of a lifeline. Without it, they would be forced to go without coverage entirely. Some in Oregon are starting to get notified about their premium increases. One family in Eugene is seeing their premiums go from $443 a month, to over $2200 a month. That's like being forced to pay a second mortgage. To Donald Trump's cabal of billionaires a few hundred or thousand dollars a month probably doesn't mean much. But for most families across Oregon and the country, that cost increase is going to mean tough decisions about whether to pay rent and buy groceries, or pay for health care. Republicans are trying to kick millions of people off their health care – again – while bending over backwards to cut taxes for billionaires and corporations. In Donald Trump's economy, billionaires win, and the rest of us lose. Simple as that. I'm committed to working to end the government shutdown and keep health care costs down. But we can and must do both. I'm not going to support any government funding bill that leaves millions of families without the health coverage they need. More soon, _Ron_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Ron Wyden instantly:

name: Michael
subject: FWD: We need to reevaluate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Dahlstrom For Governor
body:
People are angry. Let's have a frank discussion.| | | Hi there. Because this message is so important, I wanted to make sure you saw this note from Nancy. Thanks for your time! _-Michael, Team Dahlstrom_ \---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Nancy Dahlstrom** <nancy@mail.nancyforalaska.com>
Date: Wed, Nov 5, 2025
Subject: We need to reevaluate
To: <dpwillis67@gmail.com> Dear, Peter, we need to have a frank conversation. What happened last night across the country was an emergency warning to all Republicans. And if we're not careful here in Alaska, we will go the way of New York, New Jersey, and Virginia. **The Left is angry.** And they're mobilizing like we've never seen before. That's evident here in Alaska after Republicans lost Anchorage last year, Fairbanks this year, and more to come if we don't look in the mirror. They are going to erode our state from the inside out, and I have something to say about that:**EXPERIENCED
&
INCORRUPTIBLE
LEADERSHIP MATTERS!** Alaskans need to feel confident that their leadership is level-headed, knows how to navigate difficult issues, won't be bought and paid for, and has the temperament to work with all sides of the political spectrum. **Republican policies and ideals can solve our most pressing issues, but only if we can find common ground. _NOT "my way or the highway."_** Enough political games. Enough bickering. I'm ready to get to work. I'm ready to be the adult in the room. If you want this kind of leadership, please [_join_](http://links.mail.nancyforalaska.com/u/click?_t=070db738c7d848a188c7a9e8a29f1bde&_m=b5fd44131451473da397431a58c70284&_e=sWt0YPUfS1ISpAaoOH2uDvUT8ptVt2zOujorseax2oWhj1FfxtXdJNRaPfQDrzoF2zc-jmX8hOvKJBewOtp_6P-mfQvMSaVlRo8587elVuzcobyiEUeo82aaf31JNrAoxxPYn9Ywwf8Y6iuH0WqF90XB0TaJiCaePsATK54OEZbtHwZZXENSYTOSwdAq4ZtqvczRj_juvHGz2h0A_aCp_ZhGm64rlbTThnqSeLPmUlVRkONjZuQGJ0-ODCNHCK1zB2-DFx2YoQSJhpMcqZ7ve2GZst0MwMZgS0D_-y3vNgrFFp9aAlE462ITyIKj0zZJ0avCxcxlgfQgfqSSgwzo4SSZ3PQXaX-nmFTCqN-miyzMsw8eC39mz8RdbBeDUrdO7xAC_6ajD5xcKl27HEGn0goiYEFS54OLJ71YuAhPDosBroPmm1S1q68ywvB3sqlc45072rDg2Kllt1l0IXXZunh88f0TfM4XVTNizQ-Fi30VEaj6a01t_vIEps1pOTNl1mvnwsYBmJrhZ_kyrU6wbfaK9lrZUU5xmxf1eTgMaehG-_Cd1lkFuG3iw-4I3NqyKWzpJV895h2N11V-70c9hglpT3K04FsSXgSzG9fWnLuxrKX-Ip615AV0mF-_7Ncm8DujM4j1jdJ15Qyals-mjw%3D%3D)[ _my campaign_](http://links.mail.nancyforalaska.com/u/click?_t=070db738c7d848a188c7a9e8a29f1bde&_m=b5fd44131451473da397431a58c70284&_e=sWt0YPUfS1ISpAaoOH2uDvUT8ptVt2zOujorseax2oWhj1FfxtXdJNRaPfQDrzoF2zc-jmX8hOvKJBewOtp_6P-mfQvMSaVlRo8587elVuzcobyiEUeo82aaf31JNrAoxxPYn9Ywwf8Y6iuH0WqF90XB0TaJiCaePsATK54OEZbtHwZZXENSYTOSwdAq4ZtqvczRj_juvHGz2h0A_aCp_ZdELR90lMv_9ygyu3uFIGC2ThqPfqrpqZv7b3JZhwG2EwTv46fE1Tarhp0CcfVl32iTWftrlh8D_1Z_EdssKkvhIVsLBFG_585y6aAclbJqA0yWNGZl_oXEQY-FCoxlzS8G-rhSf13nYY7i_Qy9RNZ1t3yf6l2sJWnFLoFCgzzlh1UCpYIFulOMUJrmoICG7I8CA4R_OX9APqD3qL5cGivW8kykd_wsGtiO_Byl0vbtBxpyFlDTO4Vx1DD8x4-wVDi2HfvrR7T63UVbENREB4dnyOkz0dv_dWdE09jeybRfuCx-goSED-_BP6GQXDX6cSVYyusWRhKteVdAXaYLWERCQvj7foW7nKqdjwlHQTuysnTjrHf-59gaFPKCVlJHgHcJVXYv3S0cfBr-2M8gTRiLVhMsDT3CikztVM0gHfTBKiHBR06_4F4q87DKhPzOBg%3D%3D) to help me strengthen Alaska and prove that experience will lead us into the _Golden Age_.

clean_body: People are angry. Let's have a frank discussion.| | | Hi there. Because this message is so important, I wanted to make sure you saw this note from Nancy. Thanks for your time! -Michael, Team Dahlstrom \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Nancy Dahlstrom <nancy@mail.nancyforalaska.com> Date: Wed, Nov 5, 2025 Subject: We need to reevaluate To: <dpwillis67@gmail.com> Dear, Peter, we need to have a frank conversation. What happened last night across the country was an emergency warning to all Republicans. And if we're not careful here in Alaska, we will go the way of New York, New Jersey, and Virginia. The Left is angry. And they're mobilizing like we've never seen before. That's evident here in Alaska after Republicans lost Anchorage last year, Fairbanks this year, and more to come if we don't look in the mirror. They are going to erode our state from the inside out, and I have something to say about that:EXPERIENCED & INCORRUPTIBLE LEADERSHIP MATTERS! Alaskans need to feel confident that their leadership is level-headed, knows how to navigate difficult issues, won't be bought and paid for, and has the temperament to work with all sides of the political spectrum. Republican policies and ideals can solve our most pressing issues, but only if we can find common ground. NOT "my way or the highway." Enough political games. Enough bickering. I'm ready to get to work. I'm ready to be the adult in the room. If you want this kind of leadership, please to help me strengthen Alaska and prove that experience will lead us into the Golden Age. mailto:info@nancyforalaska.com?subject=I'd like to host an event!&body= mailto:info@nancyforalaska.com?subject=Question for Nancy Let's do this together, Nancy Dahlstrom Alaska's Lt. Governor & 2026 Republican Candidate for Governor | | | | | ---|---|---

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Good news on SNAP
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25]
Friends, today a federal court stepped in and ordered the USDA to provide full SNAP benefits by tomorrow.
This is very good news, but the Trump administration has already filed an appeal, so we can’t let up now. Join our fight to hold this administration accountable by chipping in now. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25]
The Trump administration has been cruelly and illegally failing more than 41 million Americans who rely on SNAP to afford groceries — so last week, I led a coalition of 25 states and the District of Columbia in filing a lawsuit to restore full funding.
Within days, two federal district courts determined that the Trump administration broke the law by choosing to suspend November SNAP benefits.
The law is clear: shutdown or not, the government must fund SNAP — and they have the funds to do it. Now, the court has ordered the USDA to do exactly that.
But the fight isn’t over — instead of getting to work delivering vital food assistance to millions of people, the USDA is focused on appealing this order.
Families who are already going hungry can’t afford more delays. We will keep taking legal action until this administration stops playing politics with people’s basic needs.
Please join this fight. Pitch in $10, $25, or whatever you can right now to power our work. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25?express_lane=true&amount=10] DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25?express_lane=true&amount=25]

Thank you for anything you can give.
—Rob

Attorney General of California

name: ICYMI News
subject: Thomas Sowell Trickle Down Theory and Tax Cuts for the Rich
body:
I hear from people all the time that Thomas Sowell is one of their heroes.

| | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zlev5tp47p20vc89ffa89mhpaooo0nqpdus0an8g_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cli62p32c5h2bs)
---
| | Dear Friend,I hear from people all the time that Thomas Sowell is one of their heroes. For those people--and you may be among them--I'm thrilled to share a digital copy of one of his commentaries with you, absolutely free: _[**_"_****_Trickle Down " Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_"_**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zog6jq34mg326g9f5ogsoc4h3msl6rhm2e8m727o_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cli62p32c5h2bs)_
---
| [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zhk539psde60d21tgj5n7hhfpnid1dfcu9jrl1ho_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cli62p32c5h2bs)
---
| | "Tax cuts for the rich" is a favorite argument of those who oppose lower tax rates. But does imposing higher tax rates on wealthy Americans actually increase the amount of revenue coming into the federal government? Hoover Institution Senior Fellow **Thomas Sowell takes an empirical approach** in his commentary, [**_"_****_Trickle Down " Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_"_**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zipv90nf0j47ikb1tlgoafseo89rcjt90nnieltg_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cli62p32c5h2bs) Sowell separates fact from fiction when it comes to tax rates and uses real life data and examples to prove that lower tax rates for all actually lead to greater revenue for the government. As a friend and supporter of the Hoover Institution, we are proud to offer you this valuable, thought-provoking resource free of charge.[**Click here to get your free copy of**** _ "Trickle Down" Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich" _****now.**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zqva4sj8695uoslbeo0i1fn89cu1v5u4hn050rc8_rp22sh2s8i6eor2cli62p32c5h2bs) Thanks,Greg Greg Stamps | _Online Development_ Hoover Institution | Stanford University
---
_With its eminent scholars and world-renowned Library and Archives, the Hoover Institution is a non-partisan, data-driven policy research organization that seeks to improve the human condition by advancing ideas that promote economic prosperity, national security, and democratic governance. Over more than one hundred years, Hoover's work has directly led to policies that have produced greater freedom, democracy, and opportunity in the United States and around the world._
---

---

# **[Ethiopia's Catholic bishops condemn deadly attack on parish, call for government protection](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9In0/IqoYKbY4M-gJT1MdHGuXwQ)**
---
Members of the Catholic Bishops’ Conference of Ethiopia (CBCE) strongly condemned attacks in the Oromia Region in late October 2025 that claimed lives and destroyed property belonging to members of the Catholic community. / Credit: World Council of Churches ACI Africa, Nov 6, 2025 / 09:00 am (CNA). Members of the Catholic Bishops’ Conference of Ethiopia (CBCE) have strongly condemned attacks in the Oromia region that claimed lives and destroyed property belonging to members of the Catholic community.In a statement issued Nov. 4, CBCE members called on the government to take urgent measures to protect citizens from further violence.

# **[Fans devastated after Dallas Cowboys player found dead at just 24: 'I am shattered'](http://trk.libertyledger._OA)**
---
Second-year defensive end Marshawn Kneeland scored the first touchdown of his career on Monday night against the Arizona Cardinals. The Dallas Cowboys player recovered a blocked punt in the end zone, and although his team lost 27-17, it was the biggest play of his career.With one sack and 12 tackles this year, the former Western Michigan player was certainly performing better statistically in his sophomore season than last year, after the Cowboys drafted him 56th overall in second round of the 2024 NFL Draft.'I still feel like she's there watching over me.'This made the Cowboys' announcement Thursday that Kneeland is now deceased even more shocking.

name: Amy Carnevale
subject: REMINDER: From Taunton to Worcester: CONSERVATIVES WIN!
body:
We notched wins up and down the ballot last night...
| | | | | [](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Sy1rpno4VLx2Je1FlB93vBOaFqJZ9s72St1Cr7xPLehi209KFYcNZOqoWQBojo-RN3KgpOkRIZ4BnBrBxaqqZi5wsKM02UAZPnLI5w5lkH_cRbfx1foNxXy9gLVCcO3qCPfTKnsiBVKnPQhBnefpIQ==&c=O9NcRxQUbWFYgYxTEG_413WpHl7dxh4sdp-6bxleBP4V3yFxYj3kMw==&ch=fNyy2lxxVwZN_4zWbSRryzFPsP2VNPtkeS9IyqgcHrDqIvpjold2rg==)
---
| Dear Peter,
Thanks to your support, we notched wins up and down the ballot last night! Across the Commonwealth, we proved that two party government is growing once again!
From city halls to school committees, Republican candidates delivered big wins that will shape the future of our communities for years to come.
🗳️ Because of you, we helped re-elect proven fighters like:
* Taunton Mayor Shaunna O'Connell
* Chelsea City Councilor Todd Taylor
* Leominster City Councilors Mark Bodanza & Thomas Ardinger
* Weymouth Town Councilors Rick Coughlin & Andrew Donovan
* Brockton City Councilor Jeffrey Thompson
* Attleboro City Councilors Jay Dilisio, Jonathan Tavares & Micheal Angelo
* Agawam City Councilor Cecelia Calabrese
* Westfield City Councilor Dan Allie
* Taunton City Councilors John McCaul & David Pottier
* Holyoke City Councilor Linda Vacon

✅ We propelled fresh voices into office including:
* Christopher Peckham, Fall River City Council
* Jaclyn Corriveau, Peabody City Council
* Will Crocker, Barnstable Town Council
* Sean Powers, Braintree Light Board

🎓 And we empowered parents and families across the state to stand up for their values, electing:
* Marc Kurzberg, Braintree School Committee
* Maura Ryan-Ciardiello, Haverhill School Committee
* James Poore & James Stors, Attleboro School Committee
* Stephen Pina, Brockton School Committee
* Ashley Dickerman, Weymouth School Committee
* Kathleen Roy, Re-elected to Worcester School Committee

....and the list goes on!
These victories didn't happen because lightning struck. They happened because of you: your donations, your time, your belief in what we're building, one victory at a time.
Today, we take a moment to celebrate our candidates. But as you know, the work doesn't stop here.
The Massachusetts Republican Party is more committed than ever to protecting these hard-fought gains, advancing our values, and winning Republican seats in every corner of the Commonwealth.
---
| With gratitude,
---
| |
---
| Amy Carnevale Chair, Massachusetts Republican Party
---
| | [SUPPORT THE MASSGOP TODAY](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Sy1rpno4VLx2Je1FlB93vBOaFqJZ9s72St1Cr7xPLehi209KFYcNZBU0__wSsEmR3O2qOu_4GqqxSGF0iwJd1Q0DMtuypf3YQiBF12A58h9W2sg0bj5DqpyEGREFPJMVMHR9NOOOlKh5cuRjNNp0vYzXCIjeUixIWk81TJI4GhufZH4E23lb99xSiTTDWxb50wLE96akMs4=&c=O9NcRxQUbWFYgYxTEG_413WpHl7dxh4sdp-6bxleBP4V3yFxYj3kMw==&ch=fNyy2lxxVwZN_4zWbSRryzFPsP2VNPtkeS9IyqgcHrDqIvpjold2rg==)

Parler

We believe that Parler has always been more than a platform — it’s a movement built by people who believe in freedom, innovation, and community. Together, we’ve helped transform Parler into a fully independent technology company serving millions of users across social, streaming, commerce, payments, and blockchain.

Now, we’re opening the door for our community to become owners in what they helped build.

The Opportunity

Parler Technologies is launching a

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=7f089051e12dd723dc4283f03a75a770bcadf66fcba9d91e9f85143971d5d9e95bd66399f42af5dafde00b75ea8e4899eb25864facfba36c06ca79967c98bf7c

Private Placement Offering

of its Series A 7% Convertible Preferred Stock, with a maximum offering amount of up to $50 million.

Investors in the Preferred Round will receive up to a 20% advertising credit in the Parler Ads Network, scaled by investment size — potentially linking participation to real marketing value and ecosystem growth.

This offering is open to accredited investors who want to participate in the expansion of the Pulse ecosystem — Parler’s next-generation platform working to connect social engagement, streaming video, e-commerce, and decentralized financial tools.

Your investment helps support the infrastructure powering this ecosystem: a fully owned stack of cloud, CDN, payments, and commerce built to ensure creators control their audience, data, and value online.

How to Learn More

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=7f089051e12dd7235c608e64339efb928a66b398cb482696eb752a7cc53ccd39965a779ccb0377de8e7acc85e792bd47815fc0f0fc61800f73b3aceabb5c2984

Click here

to join the webinar! On Thursday, November 13th, 2025 at 10:30AM CST, you can hear directly from the team, gain insight investment details, ask questions, and engage with the team behind the Movement.

You can also visit

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=7f089051e12dd723dc4283f03a75a770bcadf66fcba9d91e9f85143971d5d9e95bd66399f42af5dafde00b75ea8e4899eb25864facfba36c06ca79967c98bf7c

invest.parler.com

if you qualify as an accredited investor to explore offering details or submit your interest through our contact form.

If you’re not currently an accredited investor, you can still sign up to be notified when future participation becomes available to the broader community.

Thank you for believing in our mission — and for helping us build the infrastructure of tomorrow.

Best Regards,

Parler Technologies Inc.

Read to Invest Now?

https://click.parlermailer.com/?qs=7f089051e12dd723dc4283f03a75a770bcadf66fcba9d91e9f85143971d5d9e95bd66399f42af5dafde00b75ea8e4899eb25864facfba36c06ca79967c98bf7c

Get Started
.

Sign up for the Webinar

name: MassGOP Events
subject: Rep. Marsi Cordially Invites You to His 2026 Kickoff Thursday 11/20
body:
You are cordially invited to attend...
| | | | | [](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fNBWomzvOFjpx0OQERAutxE4bSPN3F-tcIUznQy2MPKX-tkGQp0O55KsNMOwLfQ__ZaVTcfE9a0lKXGUapUAr0xG81mvMHy-zQBhjzT7uWCZWEL0EUe-_D1IVw6AclqcAdjSwt-P651EuiqGttkKhw==&c=RbWpRCqBB5jW6Clcls5gQ9kvXUybw79tau9dH49bQpeWJnU7HdCs7Q==&ch=F-kbXQ0qTRe15mOTO04UR2EI7SreVTALW_AoBefTW6-VaQe-WFhSAw==)
---
| Hi Peter, Representative John Marsi is proud to invite you to his 2026 Campaign Kickoff, as he launches his re-election effort to continue serving the people of Massachusetts' 6th Worcester District, which includes Charlton, Dudley, Southbridge, and Spencer.
Rep. Marsi has been a strong voice for fiscal responsibility, public safety, and common-sense governance on Beacon Hill. His leadership reflects the values that Massachusetts Republicans stand for: putting taxpayers first and keeping government accountable.
Rep. John Marsi 2026 Campaign Kickoff Date: Thursday, November 20th Time: 6:00 to 8:00 PM Location: Heritage Country Club 85 Sampson Road, Charlton, MA 01507
Appetizers will be provided, and a cash bar will be available.
We hope you'll join fellow Republicans and community leaders in supporting Rep. Marsi as he kicks off his 2026 campaign and continues the work of bringing responsible leadership to our Commonwealth.
If you would like to donate, please visit: [www.RepMarsi.com/Donate](https://qgzxgy5ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fNBWomzvOFjpx0OQERAutxE4bSPN3F-tcIUznQy2MPKX-tkGQp0O59N-P1S4ao1wmVmfDRFFV0PIJbHuu3qYRn9LZ1l9F6ZE5O30Cyzux2CMLNWTKXQmUZGwnkEFMoAK3QpGsTwvldSyDLWsZNZwdIpjZLzy-BK7aV-XlXn1weg=&c=RbWpRCqBB5jW6Clcls5gQ9kvXUybw79tau9dH49bQpeWJnU7HdCs7Q==&ch=F-kbXQ0qTRe15mOTO04UR2EI7SreVTALW_AoBefTW6-VaQe-WFhSAw==).
Haley Jones Finance Director, Massachusetts Republican Party
---

name: NEGOP
subject: 🚨 Your Weekly Update From The NEGOP
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY](https://feyrpzhbb
body:
News, events, and action items to keep Nebraska red and growing strong.
| | | | |
---
| Letter from the Chair
---
Dear Fellow Republicans,
The NEGOP is proudly reinstituting our Founders' Days Dinner on December 11th. I hope many of you will join us--attending, sponsoring, and supporting our critical fundraising for 2026 Get-Out-the-Vote (GOTV) efforts. Our candidate recruitment and workshops have yielded strong results, but GOTV is the linchpin of victory. We've crafted a comprehensive plan to boost all Republican candidates up and down the ballot. Without funding, however, even the best plan is just words on paper.
November roared in like a lion. The State Treasurer's office saw swift change: Tom Briese stepped down, and Governor Jim Pillen promptly appointed Fremont Mayor Joey Spellenberg to the role. Kudos to all involved for keeping it under wraps--a rarity in Nebraska politics. Even former Senator Julie Slama was surprised, ending her brief two-day candidacy.
On a graver note, Senator Dan McKeon faces accusations of inappropriate conduct. The NEGOP will not join calls for his resignation; we trust justice to take its course. Yet I must highlight the stark contrast in responses. Republicans swiftly condemned Senator McKeon, while Democrats have been silent amid NU Regent Elizabeth O'Connor's charges of drunken driving causing injury to children. Regent O'Connor has ignored resignation demands entirely. Frankly, the GOP outcry against McKeon has been far louder than any toward O'Connor.
Why raise this now, amid my usual optimism? Tuesday's elections in blue states saw Republicans underperform compared to 2024. Voters won't rally behind a party that fails to rally behind itself. Pulling together isn't just ideal--it's essential. We must give each other the benefit of the doubt and close ranks.
Per the Nebraska Secretary of State, 622,765 registered Republicans comprise 49.5% of the state's 1,257,480 voters. With contentious 2026 races ahead and Democrats energized, county conventions leading to the primary are our chance to refocus. Let's unite against the radical Democrat agenda and build a party worth voting for.
See you December 11th--together, we win.

Mary Jane Truemper Chairman, Nebraska Republican Party
---

| WHAT'S HAPPENING...
From the legislative desk of Jeanne Reigle...
As your Government Relations Director, I'm witnessing a wave of determination sweeping through our party as we gear up for the Second Session of the 109th Legislature. With our bold Legislative Plan--adopted with resounding solidarity in August--now in hand, we're poised to champion the conservative values that define Nebraska's brighter future: from protecting life and our electoral integrity to empowering parents and securing our borders.
What excites me most? Our upcoming meetings with Republican senators, side by side with Chairman Mary Jane Truemper. These aren't just discussions--they're the spark of true collaboration, where fresh ideas ignite action and shared vision turns plans into progress. Together, we'll bridge any gaps, harness our majority, and deliver wins for families, farmers, and freedoms across the Cornhusker State. This is our moment. We're built to win, and we will--building a Nebraska where opportunity thrives and principles prevail.
Let's charge ahead with hope and heart!
Jeanne Reigle Government Relations Nebraska Republican Party govrelations[@ne.gop](mailto:political@ne.gop)

subject: The $1 Trillion CEO, Flight Cutbacks Start, and a Common Cuckoo Frenzy
body:
Tesla shareholders overwhelmingly [approved an unprecedented $1 trillion](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LEO2yAQxvGnwdtFHI5NPHjowpauHS2Mj0BDAMElUd6-SqU-QedP-knf_9d1u9qYYr79tA9a2Zct07snYqa2cSDkFlOKJTsqPsVbYPfkfVNSTYAIUm-HjemznYdj9USKxoFW1LOacJzUMoT1osfZyUUuZ4veu32RePE4O6_9jgvtQ1y_GqLUiHJSy0l7dxl37S9uWnbUXpwlHZFLO_0uMXMgnz7lRe3kymNIa2CuXYw_hDJCmfyppfN3Esp8YaEMolBGzkKZ_dljpt6FMkw9WejBNgolHdQ62FpbeRFQKhkez37vEGLn0qIDhH8loNoPVOvu9kYQqFGHd6AMgcCVZzqgFncnBg4E75gPb1MSygxtPer7S_RZi7O8PWxMfy_wf3V_repPAAAA__9ZzZl7) potential compensation package for CEO Elon Musk on Thursday, a record-setting deal he had suggested was necessary for him to remain with the company.

| | |

| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFuwyAUgOHTmC0WPAyYgaFLtnRtN4TxI6bFxiIvSX37Kr1CD_D_0vdx8ZeQS96u72FFR6n6DZ-3gkTYPC0oqOVSct0i1lTydaF4p8kDB3US4sSNn0Muhx_Y7BIioGTohNGghFRg2eK4homDjLPiJo3a2jEExDCg1TIkwVl2r5sQ3AjBFdjepDjKyaQxKjsJk7qB45yptv6r5o0WTOWoD2x9rCsrbiHab5186-Dcwfm-P2v77vef1OfawVkfw_rZWHPz_nxJbtp0A7-uIZe_nv6Ffjj4DQAA__8I7Gqy) | |
# Friday, November 7, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1962, after losing the governor’s election in California, Richard Nixon gave what he called [his “last press conference,](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGK8zAQBOCnkbsY7SqK7EJF4P9THOS640ojSytLQbGCs4nx2x--V7h2Bj6Y-b4OV5dLnqdPdyfLsQ4zrc9CzLQMnAh4yaXkOnuqseQpsX_xOKBEfQA4SDMEl8s2HJtgIxGSasiCOaEGpbFvklVKyxCkdogGSUHfR-g7N7ru1IU4hibbXQOQBkBq7FsTfadGEzuv-xFMFEdJIXNd2lvNMyeKZatvWlpf702xifnxFOos8CLwsq5ru9UXv0ba-z1x7JNQl7dQ_z7OQGG6wZf-3yw2PNZ93PNkxFFOd5fLL8l_-uFt8ScAAP__5Hhwmw)" telling reporters that “you don’t have Nixon to kick around anymore.” Six years later, he was elected president. 🎄 In yesterday’s poll, we asked if it was too early for Christmas music. 78% of our poll respondents said Nov. 1 is too early. We suspect the other 22% probably work for Big Santa. (Though we did discover after our own vigorous debate that our founder, Guy, already has his Christmas lights up.) Thanks for voting! If you’re ready to scale your next big idea without slowing down, today's sponsor, [Upwork Business Plus](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFuwyAUgOHTmC0WPAyYgaFLtnRtN4TxI6bFxiIvSX37Kr1CD_D_0vdx8ZeQS96u72FFR6n6DZ-3gkTYPC0oqOVSct0i1lTydaF4p8kDB3US4sSNn0Muhx_Y7BIioGTohNGghFRg2eK4homDjLPiJo3a2jEExDCg1TIkwVl2r5sQ3AjBFdjepDjKyaQxKjsJk7qB45yptv6r5o0WTOWoD2x9rCsrbiHab5186-Dcwfm-P2v77vef1OfawVkfw_rZWHPz_nxJbtp0A7-uIZe_nv6Ffjj4DQAA__8I7Gqy), makes it easy to hire top freelancers fast—so you can focus on growth, not guesswork.
|
