name: =?utf-8?Q?Don=E2=80=99t_f***_w/_our_courts?=
subject: Democratic PA Supreme Court Victory spurs threats of violence
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
“If you can’t beat em kill em.” This is the new strategy that many on the extreme-right, including those in positions of power, are adopting.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001cCl-YoOJA_TcZ8_03zhBNpmPHbTCEfQ0jwrT8vWMQ4J27KQLD8BzJQilVCeEzhNOeUIFeN_N52rCeWLhbj5kQwO3FQRRiJMT_4wNRxB1P_YJwg1av4nWmVc-sj1cKVwJUj5rsdq7C86nKHiGfekigMBZIAdrt85yTP0bBaYySvlnVQydZE0tMUaVBT9CitM6dyQ_NyubzEOtqzM-4Gn5mT0kepbwhZ3fXj5iJKhzAeGyUzY-QEZ9Mg==&c=pMsl3ca8uxzw8mQodQJ9vxpjxD4D0VOZynEl2pmZ5hqzNRJzTN3-iQ==&ch=teu_SgXy4tWW5KUlQNATl2fnRYCog9Gk_E_5tSXGQT8lhN_75PQOpA==)
---
| Peter,
Following MAGA's blowout defeat in the critical Pennsylvania Supreme Court the right has found themselves in an existential crisis.
Yesterday at a meeting of the Federalist Society (the far-right organization focused on judicial capture and responsible for the GOP majority on the US Supreme Court), Chad Mizelle, a former Trump DOJ appointee laid out a new approach to winning judicial fights in the aftermath of PA:
Putting down Justices like "stray cats."
---
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001cCl-YoOJA_TcZ8_03zhBNpmPHbTCEfQ0jwrT8vWMQ4J27KQLD8BzJQilVCeEzhNOeUIFeN_N52rCeWLhbj5kQwO3FQRRiJMT_4wNRxB1P_YJwg1av4nWmVc-sj1cKVwJUj5rsdq7C86nKHiGfekigMBZIAdrt85yTP0bBaYySvlnVQydZE0tMUaVBT9CitM6dyQ_NyubzEOtqzM-4Gn5mT0kepbwhZ3fb2UTkwEo3eTpxOxJmszBzQ==&c=pMsl3ca8uxzw8mQodQJ9vxpjxD4D0VOZynEl2pmZ5hqzNRJzTN3-iQ==&ch=teu_SgXy4tWW5KUlQNATl2fnRYCog9Gk_E_5tSXGQT8lhN_75PQOpA==)
---
|
This is the new strategy that many on the extreme-right, including those in positions of power, are adopting: "If you can't beat em kill em."
Already we've seen this strategy begin to play out. Just last month a South Carolina Supreme Court judge who ruled against Trump had her home burn down in a suspicious massive explosion that many suspected was arson.
The reason why they are resorting to this is because the left is making a comeback in the fight for our nation's courts. On Tuesday, Defend our Courts helped orchestrate a landslide victory that will all but guarantee Democratic control for the next decade. It sent a resounding message to MAGA: Don't f*** with our courts.
MAGA can't win these judicial fights when voters are educated on the stakes of what right-wing capture of our judiciary entails. They can try to circumvent the voters' will and win through violence but in the long run that strategy will only alienate them with the American people.
These courts are the only thing standing in the way of the full implementation of Trump's agenda and if we can keep up the pressure and keep pulling off victories in supreme court races like we just did in Pennsylvania - we will survive these next three years of Trump and reclaim America.
Winning the fight in PA required going for broke, literally, and we owe tens of thousands of dollars to our hardworking staff. [To move on to the coming judicial fights in MI, AZ, and GA, we need to close the books on PA. clean_body: “If you can’t beat em kill em.” This is the new strategy that many on the extreme-right, including those in positions of power, are adopting. | | | | | == | Peter, Following MAGA's blowout defeat in the critical Pennsylvania Supreme Court the right has found themselves in an existential crisis. Yesterday at a meeting of the Federalist Society (the far-right organization focused on judicial capture and responsible for the GOP majority on the US Supreme Court), Chad Mizelle, a former Trump DOJ appointee laid out a new approach to winning judicial fights in the aftermath of PA: Putting down Justices like "stray cats." | | | | | == | This is the new strategy that many on the extreme-right, including those in positions of power, are adopting: "If you can't beat em kill em." Already we've seen this strategy begin to play out. Just last month a South Carolina Supreme Court judge who ruled against Trump had her home burn down in a suspicious massive explosion that many suspected was arson. The reason why they are resorting to this is because the left is making a comeback in the fight for our nation's courts. On Tuesday, Defend our Courts helped orchestrate a landslide victory that will all but guarantee Democratic control for the next decade. It sent a resounding message to MAGA: Don't f*** with our courts. MAGA can't win these judicial fights when voters are educated on the stakes of what right-wing capture of our judiciary entails. They can try to circumvent the voters' will and win through violence but in the long run that strategy will only alienate them with the American people. These courts are the only thing standing in the way of the full implementation of Trump's agenda and if we can keep up the pressure and keep pulling off victories in supreme court races like we just did in Pennsylvania - we will survive these next three years of Trump and reclaim America. Winning the fight in PA required going for broke, literally, and we owe tens of thousands of dollars to our hardworking staff. == | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | == Thank you for standing beside us in PA and in the battles to come. name: Team Lizzie
subject: TOMORROW: Join Lizzie + Gavin Newsom in Houston
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
We are still celebrating Tuesday’s sweeping victories. In this year that has brought so many challenges and failures of government, this showed what’s possible when people come together and vote for change.
In California, voters stood up for fairness and democracy—saying yes to Prop 50, Governor Gavin Newsom’s Election Rigging Response Act —and against the discriminatory and destabilizing redistricting efforts we’re fighting right here in Texas as well as across the country.
That victory was the result of months of organizing and determination from people who refuse to let Trump’s redistricting power grab silence communities or dilute minority representation.
And we’re not stopping now.
Any moment now, a panel of three federal judges will decide whether Texas’ newly redrawn congressional map can be used in the 2026 elections. That ruling will determine whether Texans get the fair representation we deserve—or face another election under a map drawn to drown out the voices of Texans. Whatever happens, this fight is likely heading to the Supreme Court next.
That’s why Lizzie is joining Governor Gavin Newsom and leaders from across Texas tomorrow in Houston for a redistricting rally—and we want you to be there.
https://act.gavinnewsom.com/signup/houston-event-251108/ [https://act.gavinnewsom.com/signup/houston-event-251108/]
SIGN UP HERE [https://act.gavinnewsom.com/signup/houston-event-251108/]
Let’s show that Texans aren’t giving up or slowing down in 2026—we are showing up, standing up, and fighting back for fair maps and the representation we deserve.
Thanks,
Team Lizzie

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/folfe251107?refcode=folfe251107]

VOLUNTEER [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Ln8e8kjn2Euuh3daVlmozQ2]

P.S. Lizzie just endorsed Tom Malinowski, her good friend and classmate from the Class of 2018, who announced he’s running in the special election to fill fellow classmate Mikie Sherrill’s seat in New Jersey’s 11th Congressional District! We’re excited to help him win in what will be the first House election of 2026.
3262 Westheimer, PMB 636, Houston, TX 77098
PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Buried deep in the Appalachians, a video has exploded online—what it shows is pure nightmare fuel.

These quiet streets hold more than stories—they echo with memories that still haunt locals.

The accounts I’ve heard are unsettling—many who’ve seen it can’t look at this place the same way again.

This isn’t rumor or legend; it’s compelling evidence of something disturbing that demands attention.

After watching, you’ll understand why this story can’t be left in the dark.

[Don’t miss your chance—watch now before it’s gone.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=d3586302f47c4b74bd3a9882bcdabedf&_e=rnBdgXlQ9NLNTEATI-abPvY8U3ZdRxK9U5GFVzzBypMxXaxizATfZScs8Hz-gYxcQXoX9cIBqrswnAr53gsm1ULPHC6J1Qua-QjhUWpvB-eFVi5WFW-XVuH50j__hUqCCB6wogUJFsKa49aXqATtS63OlnXYZiYb_sE0MhQIZ0t9a8aZSauvfTErb7ziOZLckjXs3IhNXe-dKu4G_LislPe2igKsVCbzyL077KIqWhIA8B43IBlicQWrRAI-asocYXyV1HzocJwl45-LSdBQyeaS1Y2vx43r4NVb67CCTo4N4z9j2uLeyc15o4hW9mCkk0ML-v-qVgEw2Ztx5j_YU2KOHKOyK0iWs94lsLddn7HAU3teaRIbqSGQQmC7GaeUgqJW-_qBMp4PsEzNE8hksZqNHAUNAMhGoz2REaerEFJBRDchdkErTLU3o70M4rqivXymbi4Gpdl_zQeO-5XVB9eS4EQ4rqYo9HtoEKWtabzNbKhWB5ZHSH6ovKiqpCXFsSnT51D8ojp0heq8V--uIW8ObTLLCBz8GgSZysfXyClM5BjePC4NMlcz8l2QQRHy)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

We encourage you to thoroughly examine this mеssagе from our associates, as it carries substantial benefits for уоu. | | | REVEALED: Mind-Bending Footage Surfaces from Appalachian Darkness | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Buried deep in the Appalachians, a video has exploded online—what it shows is pure nightmare fuel. These quiet streets hold more than stories—they echo with memories that still haunt locals. The accounts I’ve heard are unsettling—many who’ve seen it can’t look at this place the same way again. This isn’t rumor or legend; it’s compelling evidence of something disturbing that demands attention. After watching, you’ll understand why this story can’t be left in the dark. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. name: Patrick J. Murphy
subject: From the Front with Patrick J. Murphy: Communication, Courage, and a New Generation of Leadership
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/106220/images/PJM_Logo.png]
Hi Friends,
It’s been a powerful week of reflection and forward momentum. From the big Democratic wins in this week’s elections to new conversations around leadership, communication, and service, one thing is clear: America is ready for a new generation to lead—with authenticity, accountability, and heart.
In the Media: Why Leaders Cannot Outsource Communication
This week, I was honored to be featured in Forbes , where I spoke about why leaders cannot outsource communication. Whether you’re running a platoon, a company, or a country, your words and how you deliver them shape trust. The same truth applies today: people don’t follow memos; they follow leaders. Delegating your voice creates a vacuum, and in a vacuum, others define the story for you. I shared lessons learned from my time in the Army and Congress, and how today’s leaders must lead from the front, not delegate their voice. In an age of noise and uncertainty, the most powerful thing a leader can do is show up, speak clearly, candidly, and often. Because clarity, like trust, compounds.
Read the full Forbes column here. [https://www.forbes.com/councils/forbescommunicationscouncil/2025/11/06/why-leaders-cannot-outsource-communication/]
On Substack: Another Change Election
In my latest Substack post, I discuss this week’s election results and what they say about where America is likely headed for the midterms in 2026. Politics is cyclical, and voters made a clear statement in rebuke of the current Trump administration. As I write in my piece, “that these elections happened against the backdrop of a government shutdown makes it all the more significant.” Read the full column on Substack. [https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/another-change-election?r=5e3684&utm_medium=ios]
Big Wins in PA
I couldn’t be prouder that Army veterans Danny Ceisler and Siddiq Kamara—two candidates endorsed by my PAC, Kennedy Dems PA—won their races for county sheriff in Bucks County and Delaware County, respectively. We also got a clean sweep for DA, row officers, and judges, including Tiffany Thomas-Smith, a military mom who made history as the first Black woman ever elected judge in Bucks County.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP25/1/106220/images/D.%20Ceisler%20Photo.png]
At Danny Ceisler’s victory party for Bucks County Sheriff with him and his father, Larry.
On Warrior Money: A Conversation with Secretary Pete Buttigieg
This week’s episode of Warrior Money is one of my favorites yet. I sat down with Secretary Pete Buttigieg to talk about leadership, purpose, and the lessons that military service brings to public life. His perspective on resilience, responsibility, and results-driven leadership is exactly what our national conversation needs right now.
Watch the full episode of Warrior Money with Pete Buttigieg here. [https://youtu.be/LlgYrWLdeT0?si=sMdXPkX4DSAv2ByX]
Continuing the Mission: Bridging Public Service and Private Innovation
As always, my mission remains to strengthen the connection between public service and private innovation. Whether it’s educating investors through Hilco Global’s Geopolitical work, supporting veteran entrepreneurs, or mentoring the next generation of civic leaders, I’m constantly reminded that service isn’t a chapter—it’s a calling.
Together, we can build stronger communities, more accountable institutions, and a leadership culture worthy of the people it serves.
Thank you for being part of the journey.
Hon. Patrick J. Murphy
32nd Army Under Secretary

If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/JcTKjLm2l0SduvIvv8SeUg2?unsubscribedata=OSVNw7lsCPobjP%2FLcz5uGLL3cLsXOiR2oWQh9tuc0VLxkDmQuWU0UUI8IVcQowaiEmFhQe053ibW22Rg3GhMSnxL2KtbiSeF9I7y7SmXlXAl6oGXP1Sxl%2FTuLv3PAg%2FSB1CnR9H6PNQ69X5XgRVYCjPd9hEyfxoCHQz99is7P3EXYCPNe385oEHFElUoDbk0KBM0zQO%2BSDhmICXWv2WETm6uTZFN60%2B5vuPdm9xYKSzZzr4J0EHl08aFDcmLSk014qUn3f03SC%2FhcTKgZrpdJgZZ%2B1fyph3R0gQXYkZfopILUEXzfyerFtr8Rtg2ivmxflkRl0blZAKxXpsBEW1Ce5k18KAmRVGPdvdQC%2F35fsWJgJVFGi1P5H9Vsny%2FakwQP2BHK4Q79FyamcsJlccG5qVoAKPuR9kYQnS51fmJeBMpGby%2Fesh0X8XrDzWQD%2BU8 .

Peter,

Trust fund liberal Jon Ossoff has $21 MILLION in the bank to keep Georgia blue.

Look at the numbers even closer and one thing is crystal clear: **[His biggest backers aren’t hardworking Georgians and Americans.](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/dGXzm8rjK6GNC7pPZ3QomdHrEJ77BmSmPP5GCXkJOk002TAmC0y1RIC_jcuUV1UNb4Q-jyU7yPz_ewuTlSOefCmuRam7M2UUQxA3qQQdoyD7Ksl1IPdrOL5h8QtrYyaVxCgcFYPBRW_kAnn8WPdfyy71_HL37gLKIlfI7hR42cXlQWAxO-D_sLMpzRp8Aymg9ZT7zOiQCKNGch5H-kExDVz9k1Mb_OVtnPeRCRTY4XA_8mUuCXpB2RztJghZr_Jc4Ejme3Fb2Na-s0FTT7JTUSosGKdJLQrvirwa9M8QMR0xu_rmooIaEz1dwjwY6s3E6P0i20kuAQGIwyyDqv37k_HhPwT-msv3Ys1n-bAefkY7AmKw6yZ0KNFd7Ro-1urXGYfDHCeJFgJk6E9GRDkcKVOQkZu_f5STklr9Glz1Iu9ayzHgCc0paDyKIE4JVZqtEyfTQb1GR2KhmaQSBfnX1QYtsnup89-3lIc4nKqSUwXCrQXDHVZFOaFXcQp1aNVoHDaUAcJYmLAKmQjpQO9Y5d3wFomfZUGZWPwKPlC2kq7RILRa7daA8di5d_8ephKU2sqQK7rtMLtsUXCl17caKRgKydYej_pW9DaSpqJo1hnJlIygZ-ZAN_dgUMfabnSjgb9d612I_Zbq6O3f2fooVk8J4pktry_EqTGsUMUYpn9VQogw3LYWfVtAysToW4PtRgwp27OqcttCgw_qBTVeBlGr2vN_9p0i1sbIifPhswA7kTYRPOPw2RcADGVmykefaB51FWici0Bt7hgU1BQy1LH02Fw1wkkH5YEKa5rx5mztxUo7fuBpsYnGECFOsGZdPZRqkLTKCV6CmowUkpezd27JCi93poeSOa1LvH1B8Hhz3JKyfKKU1CIn8QdLiW0ej4TxL66wiNdKS68KyXizv3tnHoFQgDuLKBZpZ1Rd-PSig80LnxYsTrJJwUinkZr_pg9_yIpt1p_6EZfp8R_q2D1KuDG8K_wxp8hgUo7j_kGHWemPRqSloa9uAQ/qRyRQ0LGn0Z0fE4BsKkzjX9moOAVVqAM/14)**

name: Adelita Grijalva
subject: Speaker Johnson’s snarky comment about me
body:
I know you’re likely getting a ton of emails from candidates and causes right now, Peter.
But I have to ask: Is there anything I can say to convince you to chip in $25 between Real Texas PAC and my Senate campaign in Ohio? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
What if I told you that right after I joined this race, experts at The Cook Political Report increased our odds of winning?
What if I told you that CNN and The New York Times report that Ohio is “in play” – but that corporate special interests are gearing up to spend millions against me?
What if I told you that misleading attack ads have already hit the airwaves – and that whether we’re able to counter these smears and reach voters with the truth depends on people like you?
This grassroots, small-dollar donor movement is our best chance to flip Ohio’s Senate seat. But we’re facing an uphill battle, and I need folks like you by my side to win.
So please, split $25 or more between Real Texas PAC and me to power our campaign forward, win in Ohio, and keep up the fight for working people. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Sherrod Brown and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: Elise Stefanik sold out the healthcare of 17 million Americans….
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
And now she’s running for NY governor.
#
Elise Stefanik sold out the healthcare of 17 million Americans as part of a failed bid to be Trump’s UN ambassador – and now she’s running for NY governor.
New York deserves so much better than someone who would gut Medicaid and Medicare for their own career ambitions.
That’s why this summer, Rep. Paul Tonko and I hosted a town hall in her district: to organize people to support one another. And we are going to win.
[](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251107.AcXlT5&refcode=em20251107-10163-P_FND_RES_HDL&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10163%2E2526796%2E_GJNWx)
[Will you make a $5 contribution to power our fight on the road and fight for a better future for all?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251107.AcXlT5&refcode=em20251107-10163-P_FND_RES_HDL&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=10163%2E2526796%2E_GJNWx)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20251107.AcXlT5&refcode=em20251107-10163-P_FND_RES_HDL&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=10163%2E2526796%2E_GJNWx)**
Whether you can donate today or not, thank you so much for being a part of this movement.
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20251107?refcode=em20251107-10163-P_FND_RES_HDL&t=22&refcode2=10163_2526796__GJNWx&akid=10163%2E2526796%2E_GJNWx)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20251107-10163-P_FND_RES_HDL&t=23&akid=10163%2E2526796%2E_GJNWx)**

name: Team GIFFORDS
subject: Catastrophic
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
#

Team,
**House Republicans are preparing to pass a bill for a national concealed carry mandate. The bill could be introduced any day now — and the GOP is signaling that they have the votes.**
This would be catastrophic. If Republicans pass a national concealed carry mandate, it would force states to allow people to carry hidden, loaded guns anywhere, without meeting state or local safety standards.
That means someone from a state with no rules about who can buy or carry weapons could come into your town, neighborhood, or business — and there’s nothing you could do to stop them.
This is a real emergency, but there is still time to fight back.
Our team at GIFFORDS is working to build the largest mobilization program since Donald Trump took office. We will be running an aggressive outreach and advertising campaign to counter the NRA’s lies and put pressure on lawmakers to vote against this dangerous bill.
All of this will take a lot of resources, which is why we are humbly asking:
[**Will you please chip in any amount today to help GIFFORDS PAC defeat the GOP’s nationwide concealed carry mandate? 100% of your donations will go towards protecting more lives from gun violence.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-q42025.UDsFRy&refcode=em251107-1-b&t=1&akid=11027%2E3935704%2EcpILKy)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_

name: Cate Paterson (Team Walz)
subject: Where things stand
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
#
Peter,
Cate from Team Walz here — I wanted to reach out and keep you posted on where our fundraising numbers are before we hit the end of November, and as we approach the end of 2025.
Since we’ve launched this campaign, Governor Walz has seen an outpouring of support — we’ve hit each and every goal we’ve set.
But I wanted to let you know what the end of the year will look like for our campaign.
We’re going to be reaching our one and only public deadline of 2025 on December 31, and it’s going to be here before you know it. **This will be the first chance to show Minnesotans just how strong our campaign is.**
Our opponents are going to be combing through our numbers searching for weaknesses, but with you on this team, I know they’ll only find strengths.
**[Will you donate $5 to help make sure that we start on the right track as we head towards our first public deadline?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251107.3dXym0&amount=5&refcode=em20251107-55&t=1&akid=55%2E34582%2EYa3kQe)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251107.3dXym0&amount=5&refcode=20251107-55&t=20&akid=55%2E34582%2EYa3kQe)**
With appreciation,
Cate Paterson
Finance Director
Tim Walz for Governor

Under Governor Walz’s leadership, Minnesota is showing the world what’s possible when we come together with grit, compassion, and a shared sense of purpose.
We secured free school meals for every student, cut child poverty, passed Paid Family Leave, delivered record investments in education, and more.
**[Now we need your help to stop MAGA Republicans from dragging us backward. Pitch in $5 today to help us win this race and keep moving forward — together.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251107-55&t=21&refcode2=55_34582_Ya3kQe&akid=55%2E34582%2EYa3kQe)**

Email us: [info@walzforgovernor.org](mailto:info@walzforgovernor.org)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.

name: The Democrats
subject: re: Donald Trump attacking American farmers
body:
---
Derek, we wanted to make sure you saw this: We traveled to Marengo, Illinois to hear first-hand from John Bartman, of Bartman Family Farm, about how Trump's trade wars have hurt American soybean farmers. Here’s what he had to say: [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025-farmers?refcode=em_20251107_5197_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5197_22930333_Z-Xh44&akid=5197%2E22930333%2EZ-Xh44) Sadly, it’s not just soybean farmers who are feeling the pain. American cattle ranchers are being devastated by the news that **Donald Trump plans to quadruple beef imports from Argentina,** undercutting American ranchers to help bail out one of his favorite world leaders. [**Donald Trump doesn’t care if he kills American family farms. But Democrats do, and we don’t plan on letting him bankrupt the people who grow America’s food. Please check out our latest video today, and then chip in $7 to help us make more content like this, fight for all Americans, and take back Congress in 2026 →**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025-farmers?amount=7&refcode=em_20251107_5197_fr&refcode2=5197_22930333_Z-Xh44&akid=5197%2E22930333%2EZ-Xh44) **[WATCH THE VIDEO](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2025-farmers?amount=7&refcode=em_20251107_5197_fr&refcode2=5197_22930333_Z-Xh44&akid=5197%2E22930333%2EZ-Xh44)** Thank you, The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: The Grant Institute
subject: Grant Report: November 7, 2025
body:
Election News Update from the Grant Institute

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/a75ace589753/grant-report-september-12-12770056?e=c16f400a56)
---
|
---
| | | | | |
**On Election Day and Teachable Moments****By Justin Riemer, President, USGI, and Marshal Trigg, Junior Counsel, USGI**
On Tuesday, millions of Americans in several states voted in contests ranging from governorships to local initiatives. The process went smoothly for most voters. But not everything went off without a hitch. A spate of bomb threats briefly disrupted voting in one [Pennsylvania](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=8b7edf682c&e=c16f400a56) polling place and several in New Jersey, leading to an [arrest](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d1de831502&e=c16f400a56) and a [court](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=caa4fbcd5f&e=c16f400a56) extending polling hours in Passaic County.
Then there were the unforced errors like in Chester County, Pennsylvania. Shortly after polls opened, election officials [discovered](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=9641105e1b&e=c16f400a56) they had omitted 75,000 unaffiliated and third-party voters—about 19 percent of the county’s electorate—from all poll books.
To their credit, county officials appeared to respond diligently by alerting the Department of State, deploying supplemental poll books, getting a court order to extend polling hours, and communicating their response to county voters and the public.
Poll workers also reportedly followed proper protocol by offering provisional ballots instead of turning voters away or letting them vote regular ballots. The county [s](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=7aa3a1c348&e=c16f400a56)[tated](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=af8ee222ff&e=c16f400a56) there were sufficient provisional ballots available, although some precincts ran out of provisional envelopes. The county [reports](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=6c69be22e6&e=c16f400a56) that voters cast over 12,100 provisional ballots, certainly enough to strain local polling locations.
Overall, this was an orderly response, especially compared to another recent breakdown that occurred in [Luzerne County](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=3e718d67b2&e=c16f400a56) in the 2022 election.
But the damage was done. Fixing a countywide error across 230 polling locations while voting is underway is a real challenge. It took until 3:45 p.m. to deploy all the supplemental poll books. Chester voters who dutifully showed up only to find their names missing were understandably unsettled by the inconvenience. And the incident was bound to feed suspicion, particularly given that the system error had categorically excluded certain voters on the basis of party affiliation (or lack thereof).
The error reportedly [originated](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=3cc12ac3fd&e=c16f400a56) in the poll book extract, i.e., the database query that generates precinct-level voter lists. Somehow, that process only extracted voters registered with one of the two major parties, suggesting it may have been a setting used for primary elections.
Whether a software glitch, user error, or some combination of both, the issue was preventable. For instance, Alleghany County avoids such mishaps by conducting a “[spot check](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d561e7ec12&e=c16f400a56)[”](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=760241f232&e=c16f400a56) before printing paper poll books. This or other simple quality control processes would have likely caught the error. The incident also raises questions about why SURE, the state’s voter registration system, allows a county to extract an incomplete list in a general election open to all voters – or at the very least, why it wouldn’t display a warning message.
Chester also uses paper instead of electronic pollbooks (“e-poll books”). Fayette County's [experience](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=b3dc327960&e=c16f400a56) on Tuesday shows that e-pollbooks do not necessarily guarantee accuracy. The county said the Pennsylvania Department of State sent it the 2024 voter file for use in e-pollbooks instead of the current 2025 data. E-poll books would have at least made it easier for Chester to provide supplemental lists to polling locations quicker than printing them.
Chester has [committed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=afb628b0e1&e=c16f400a56) to conducting a “detailed and comprehensive . . . investigation.” That’s a good start, but the review should be conducted independently and thoroughly examine all root causes, including any oversight lapses by the county election board and Department of State.
There’s a delicate balance between piling on and ensuring accountability. The latter doesn’t equate to discrediting our elections or election officials. These are consequential mistakes because they risk disenfranchising voters, eroding voter confidence, and providing fodder for conspiracy theorists.

| | **Arkansas **
The Secretary of State's office found approximately [240 potential noncitizens](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=ec1adfaf66&e=c16f400a56) listed on the state’s voter registration rolls after comparing the voter rolls with federal citizenship verification data. Letters have been mailed to the identified voters requesting proof of citizenship, and local election officials will be notified of the office’s findings.
**California **
The U.S. Department of Justice [deployed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=1751b2b028&e=c16f400a56) poll monitors to polling locations in five California counties during Tuesday’s elections.
Voters on Tuesday passed [Proposition 50](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=59db8a0ecf&e=c16f400a56) which amends the California Constitution to allow the legislature to adopt a new congressional district map for 2026 through 2030. Republicans [sued](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=c01a3fbe94&e=c16f400a56) the next day arguing the map is an unconstitutional racial gerrymander.
**Georgia**
In [Chatham County](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=716c922b1b&e=c16f400a56), election officials reported that an unknown number of absentee ballots from a batch of 533 total processed by an out-of-state vendor were lost in the mail. The county sent replacement ballots to affected voters.
**Hawaii**
The bipartisan state elections commission [voted](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=82a3a86956&e=c16f400a56) 5-3 to request that the Legislature ban mail-in and early voting and return to in-person only voting on Election Day with an ID, while limiting absentee ballots to military families and voters with special needs.
**Kansas**
Attorney General Kris Kobach [announced](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=9690e4af59&e=c16f400a56) the filing of [six felony counts](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=a3e7f7fe12&e=c16f400a56) against Coldwater Mayor Jose Ceballos: three counts for voting without being qualified and three counts for election perjury. The charges, which were announced one day after Ceballos was reelected, allege that he voted in Kansas elections despite not being a U.S. citizen. The [Attorney General](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e4cbef3a66&e=c16f400a56) states that his office expects to find more illegally registered voters.
**Maine**
On November 4, voters [rejected](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=50db5e55fd&e=c16f400a56) Question 1, a citizen initiative that would have mandated photo ID to vote and changed multiple absentee voting rules, including limiting drop-boxes and ending phone application requests. The measure was defeated by 63.9% to 36.1% of votes counted.
Incarcerated Mainers retain the right to vote via absentee ballot, the state being [one of only two](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=085c3b126f&e=c16f400a56) states to permit imprisoned felons to vote.
In Brewer, an “[honest mistake](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=ef2c6bf0e0&e=c16f400a56)” by a UPS employee resulted in 250 absentee-ballot packets for the November 4 election being sent to a private residence in Newburgh instead of the intended destination. The Secretary of State’s Office confirmed that one of two boxes delivered to the municipality of Ellsworth on September 30 was missing five packets (totaling 250 ballots) and noted that detectives retrieved the ballots from the town office in Newburgh. Because of the incident, the office announced it will no longer use UPS or the United States Postal Service for handling the remainder of the 2025 ballots.
**Maryland**
Governor Moore has [announced](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=a10dfe2a7f&e=c16f400a56) the creation of a “Governor’s Redistricting Advisory Commission” to consider mid-cycle redrawing of the state’s congressional map ahead of the 2026 elections. The move comes despite opposition from the state Senate president.
The [Annapolis City Council](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=43cfb3bead&e=c16f400a56) voted to table consideration of adopting ranked-choice voting, putting on hold discussion which had sought to introduce the alternative voting method for future municipal elections.
**Montana**
A [new law](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=67be00323d&e=c16f400a56) requires voters using mail-in ballots to [include their birth year](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=47091fd6cb&e=c16f400a56) next to their signature on the ballot envelope, and any ballots without that detail are subject to rejection. County election offices in Montana say that [hundreds of ballots](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e3293892f4&e=c16f400a56) ([over 700 in one county](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=0c44a116fa&e=c16f400a56)) are being flagged under the rule, and officials are ramping up outreach and cure efforts.
**New Jersey**
[ On Election Day](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=1e4a263df7&e=c16f400a56), the [Attorney General](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=4ce2b1d85c&e=c16f400a56) reported that multiple polling locations in seven counties were temporarily evacuated or redirected after receiving bomb threats. Law enforcement investigated and determined that the threats were not credible. The state cleared some polling places, while redirecting some voters to alternate polling locations, and allowing polling locations to [remain open later](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=fe021c2124&e=c16f400a56).
The U.S. Department of Justice [deployed federal election monitors](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=a1b1c4ade3&e=c16f400a56) to key polling sites in Passaic County during Tuesday’s election. The deployment follows the state Republican Party raising concerns about mail-ballot security and alleged prior irregularities. The New Jersey Attorney General [called](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5fb3cb32a0&e=c16f400a56) the deployment “highly inappropriate.” No incidents regarding the deployment were publicly reported on Election Day.
**Ohio**
Secretary of State LaRose has referred for review [more than 1,200](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=4ddc3b83d3&e=c16f400a56) potential election-related violations to the U.S. Department of Justice, including cases involving possible noncitizen registrations, double voting, and ballot-harvesting. The Secretary said he [referred over 633 cases](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2c22811b49&e=c16f400a56) of suspected voter fraud to county prosecutors, with only 12 being prosecuted.
Secretary LaRose [publicly endorsed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2a25efc6e2&e=c16f400a56) Senate Bill 153, a proposal requiring proof of U.S. citizenship for voter registration. He also endorsed Senate Bill 293, which would require the receipt of absentee by election day, noting the change would align Ohio with about two-thirds of states.
As of November 4, [Cuyahoga County](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=fb05090a79&e=c16f400a56) reported that roughly 17,000 mail-in ballots (nearly 30% of those requested for the general election) had not been returned by Election Day. This would be its lowest return rate in ten years.
A candidate running for the Talawanda City Board of Education was removed from his role as a precinct election official after the board found he was working at a polling location [in the same precinct where he was on the ballot](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=b28b9d01ee&e=c16f400a56). The candidate, who had previously served as a poll worker at that location for six elections, left the site promptly once notified of the violation. The Board of Elections noted the incident is under review and that it was implementing corrective procedures to prevent similar errors.
**Oregon**
Officials announced they will not pursue [criminal investigations](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=907687f10b&e=c16f400a56) for individuals who cast ballots they were not eligible to vote. The elections director noted the cases were administrative mistakes by Oregon DMV workers who erroneously registered the voters.
**Pennsylvania**
[Attorney General Sunday](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=9d78bf054a&e=c16f400a56) announced criminal charges against six canvassers and one field-director for submitting falsified voter registration forms ahead of the 2024 general election in multiple counties.
Cambria County election officials invited the public to a [public testing event](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=102f680795&e=c16f400a56) for voting equipment, where voters can fill out sample ballots and witness the tallying of results.
As noted above, [in Chester County](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=079daf00c9&e=c16f400a56), a poll book extract error omitted third-party and unaffiliated voters, prompting use of provisional ballots, creation of supplemental poll-books, and an order to keep polling places open until 10:00 p.m. In Dauphin County, a [bomb threat](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e06d92b755&e=c16f400a56) at a polling site caused about a 20-minute suspension of operations and turned away approximately 20 voters before reopening under county supervision.
[In York County](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=30407c7544&e=c16f400a56), election officials have implemented a new technology-driven system for over-the-counter ballot requests, and the results are striking. Previously, voters requesting ballots in person often faced wait times of 25-30 minutes. With the new system in place, wait times have fallen to around 5-10 minutes.
[Three individuals](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=42bcbed0f1&e=c16f400a56) were charged in a voter registration-fraud investigation in Monroe County after allegedly submitting false voter registration and absentee ballot applications in 2024. Officials say the alleged fraud involved forged signatures and fictitious information.
[In Fayette County](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5b2097d5ec&e=c16f400a56), the Department of State mistakenly sent the 2024 voter file to election officials instead of the updated 2025 file, causing some electronic poll books to incorrectly mark voters as having already cast a ballot. Officials switched to backup paper poll books once it discovered the error.
**Tennessee**
Secretary of State Hargett announced that his office identified [42 individuals](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=7308139e21&e=c16f400a56) flagged as possible noncitizens. Secretary Hargett said these cases will be referred to the FBI for further investigation and hailed the state’s use of the federal Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE) system to screen the electorate.
**Texas**
[Voters approved](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2dfa278dde&e=c16f400a56) Proposition 16 on November 4, adding a constitutional amendment that explicitly requires U.S. citizenship to vote in state and local elections.
In [Nueces County](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=9915d1c02d&e=c16f400a56), voters discovered that two constitutional amendment items, Propositions 14 and 15, appeared on the ballot out of their numerical order (jumping from 13 to 15, then back to 14). County officials acknowledged the error, explained it stemmed from a formatting issue that was not caught by the logic and accuracy testing board.
**Wyoming**
Over the [past two years](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e43f2e21f0&e=c16f400a56), the Secretary of State received 80 formal election-related complaints, with six (about 7.5%) alleging possible voter fraud. The bulk of complaints instead focused on errors or misconduct by election officials, campaign mailers, or improper campaigning.

| | **Georgia**
The State Election Board will meet on [November 12](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=c9add3f20c&e=c16f400a56) and the agenda includes a presentation on cybersecurity and an update on pending investigative matters in a report from the Secretary of State’s office. The State Election Board [will also meet](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2ed6393448&e=c16f400a56) on December 9 and 10. The Board will consider proposed rules relating to the use of voting equipment during its December 10 meeting: [Subject 183-1-12-.02 (Definitions)](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=795d268f52&e=c16f400a56)[.](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=1fdbe1f54d&e=c16f400a56)
**Kentucky**
The State Board of Elections has filed over [30 administrative regulations](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=6d4338a540&e=c16f400a56) with the state’s Legislative Research Commission.
**Michigan**
Michigan has multiple pending rulemakings nearing completion. They include:
* [2025-13 ST](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=45a76176c5&e=c16f400a56): Voter Registration Cancellation, Challenge, and Correction
* [2025-14 ST](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e123dde1b9&e=c16f400a56): Use of Electronic Pollbook
* [2025-15 ST](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=faf1d8204c&e=c16f400a56): Election Challengers and Poll Watchers
* [2025-52 ST](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=945c25407f&e=c16f400a56): Disqualification from Ballot Based Upon Contents of Affidavit of Identity

**Nevada**
The Secretary of State’s Office is holding [two in-person public meetings](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d6ddd78061&e=c16f400a56) to receive feedback on its Elections Procedure Manual. The first meeting was scheduled for [November 6](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5ff0642340&e=c16f400a56) and covered Chapters 3 (Elections), 4 (Candidates and Campaigns), and 5 (Petitions). The second meeting will be held today ([November 7](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=87b85c73c2&e=c16f400a56)) at 9:00 a.m., focusing on Chapters 6 (City Clerks), 7 (Election Security), and Appendix A.
**Oregon**
A proposed [rule](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d7ab643bfe&e=c16f400a56) implements legislative mandates found in HB 4133 (2022) and requires the electronic voter registration system to allow a qualified person to complete and deliver a registration card electronically by providing the last four digits of their Social Security number and an image of their signature.
**Washington**
In accordance with HAVA, the rule [amendments](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=73a07c74c7&e=c16f400a56) clarify that a voter who asserts the signature on a ballot declaration is not theirs prior to 8:00 PM on Election Day is entitled to vote a provisional ballot, and provisional ballots are subject to investigation per RCW 29A.60.195. The regulation previously entitled a voter to receive a replacement ballot.

| | **Arizona**
_**Santa Cruz County v. Audit USA**_ , No. 2 CA-CV 2024-0328 (Ariz. Ct. App. Div. 2)
On October 20, the Arizona Court of Appeals [issued](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e2c80cb1b5&e=c16f400a56) a unanimous decision holding that a county may not preemptively sue a public-records requester simply for making a request, even when the county fears litigation. The county had preemptively brought litigation in an attempt to force the courts to determine whether cast vote records were a public record.
_**Heap v. Galvin**_ , No. CV2025-020621 (Ariz. Super. Ct. Maricopa Cnty.)
On November 6, a state court [denied](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=966c6bed33&e=c16f400a56) Maricopa County Recorder Justin Heap’s petition to halt a third party audit of the county’s voter registration and IT systems. Heap had argued he was excluded from key decisions and that sharing sensitive voter-registration data with the third party group raises security concerns. The lawsuit, which was filed five months ago, arises from a dispute in Arizona’s largest county over who controls election infrastructure. A hearing on the merits is set for January 2026.
**Arkansas**
_**Democratic Party of Arkansas v. Sanders**_ , No. 60CV-25-12782 (Ark. Cir. Ct. Pulaski Cnty.)
On October 31, a state court [ordered](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=1ae08cd211&e=c16f400a56) that the special election to fill the vacant House District 70 seat must be held on March 3, 2026, rejecting the governor’s claims of complete discretion over setting special election dates. The court cited Arkansas law requiring a governor to fill a vacancy within 150 days unless this was “impracticable or duly burdensome.”
_**Evans v. Harrison**_ , No. 12CV-24-209 (Ark. Cir. Ct. Cleburne Cnty.)
On October 30, the Arkansas Supreme Court [struck down](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=0d4d38f7a6&e=c16f400a56) two state statutes governing the timeframe for county-level initiative petitions. The Court held that the laws conflicted with the Arkansas Constitution, which mandates that local initiative petitions be filed no earlier than 90 days and no later than 60 days before the election.
**California**
_**People of the State of California v. City of Huntington Beach**_ , No. 30-2024-01393606-CU-WM-NJC (Cal. Super. Ct. Orange Cnty.)
On November 3, a state appellate court [struck down](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=755bdc775c&e=c16f400a56) Huntington Beach’s voter ID measure, finding it conflicted with state election law prohibiting cities from implementing voter ID requirements. The measure would have required city voters to show ID at the polls beginning in 2026.
**District of Columbia**
_**LULAC v. Executive Office of the President**_ , No. 1:25-cv-00946 (D.D.C.)
On October 31, 2025, the U.S. District Court for D.C. [permanently blocked](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=819bdb602d&e=c16f400a56) the presidential directive requiring documentary proof of U.S. citizenship on the federal voter-registration form. The court cited separation of powers doctrine, stating that the U.S. Constitution assigns the regulation of elections to Congress.
**Michigan**
_**United States v. Benson**_ , No. 1:25-cv-01148 (W.D. Mich.)
On October 30, 2025, the League of Women Voters of Michigan [filed a motion to intervene](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=ef0e0e5df3&e=c16f400a56) in the federal suit in which the U.S. Department of Justice is suing the State of Michigan and Secretary of State Benson for refusing to turn over voter registration data.
**Nebraska**
_**State ex rel. Hilgers v. Wyss**_ , No. __ (Neb. Dist. Ct. Madison Cnty. 2025)
On November 5, Nebraska Attorney General Mike Hilgers [announced](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d8904b0dd3&e=c16f400a56) that the state had filed suit against Swiss national Hansjörg Wyss and six Wyss-backed entities, alleging violation of Nebraska’s 2022 statute that prohibits foreign nationals from making direct or indirect contributions to ballot question committees.
**New Jersey**
_**New Jersey Democratic State Committee v. Board of Elections of Bergen County**_ , No. BER-L-7347-25 (N.J. Super. Ct. Law Div.)
On November 3, 2025, the New Jersey Democratic State Committee (NJDSC) [sued](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=c28f130ff7&e=c16f400a56) the Bergen County Board of Elections after approximately 300 mail-in ballots submitted without the required inner secrecy envelope (i.e., “naked ballots”) were flagged for potential rejection.
_**Republican National Committee v. Burlington County Board of Elections**_ , No. BUR-L-002476-25 (N.J. Super. Ct. Law Div.)
On October 24, the Republican National Committee [sued](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=ae6a1b3514&e=c16f400a56) the Burlington County Board of Elections, alleging violations of New Jersey’s Open Public Records Act (OPRA) and common-law right of access after officials refused to release video footage of ballot drop boxes and related chain-of-custody logs. The complaint can be found [here](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=8a1078a553&e=c16f400a56).
**New York**
_**New York Republican State Committee v. State of New York**_ , No. 2:25-CV-6083 (E.D.N.Y.)
On October 30, 2025, the New York Republican State Committee, joined by county and town governments and local elected officials, [filed a federal lawsuit](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=f4998f8bd6&e=c16f400a56) challenging a 2023 statute which moves most town and county elections in New York from odd- to even-numbered years.
**Virginia**
_**NAACP Virginia State Conference v. O’Bannon**_ , No. 1:25-cv-01937 (E.D. Va.)
On October 31, 2025, the NAACP Virginia State Conference and the Advancement Project [filed a federal lawsuit](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=9952cd7405&e=c16f400a56) against state and local election officials. The complaint alleges that Virginia registrars are unlawfully rejecting voter registration applications from college students because the forms lack dormitory names, room numbers, or campus mailbox details. The lawsuit contends these omissions are immaterial under federal law.
**Wisconsin **
_**Cerny v. Wisconsin Elections Commission**_ , No. 24-cv-1353 (Wis. Cir. Ct. Waukesha Cnty.)
On October 31, a state court [granted a stay](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=c16ddc0eab&e=c16f400a56) of the same voter verification requirement the court had ordered just a few weeks earlier. The October 3 order had directed the Wisconsin Elections Commission to verify the citizenship status of all registered voters in the state, and to halt accepting any new voter registration applicants without citizenship verification. The Wisconsin Department of Justice asked the court on October 6 to suspend the decision pending a statewide election in February.

| | The administration is pursuing a plan to launch a [national online version](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=9353cb76eb&e=c16f400a56) of the federal voter-registration form, which would include identity verification and citizenship checks via the U.S. Department of Homeland Security before forwarding registrations to states. State election officials have voiced concerns, citing potential conflicts with their election laws and data-privacy obligations, although EAC Chairman Don Palmer noted the existing form would remain available.
The Department of Homeland Security has [proposed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=a348479a3d&e=c16f400a56) expanding its Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE) to include Social Security and driver’s license data for potential citizenship verification of voters. A copy of the applicable Federal Register notice is available [here](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=3c14693628&e=c16f400a56).

---
|
---
| | A [new article](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=47338e75b0&e=c16f400a56) highlights how Virginia election officials emphasize that while the technical mechanics of elections, including accurate voter rolls, risk-limiting audits, and poll-worker training are essential, the real challenge lies in maintaining public trust. It calls on leaders across the political spectrum to visibly endorse the non-partisan “[Principles for Trusted Elections](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=ded1ce8cbf&e=c16f400a56),” and argues that credible elections depend not only on process integrity but also on broad perception that the system is fair and transparent.
An [o](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=4113490490&e=c16f400a56)[pinion piece](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=7dc705529f&e=c16f400a56) by John Hendrickson and Hans von Spakovsky in The American Spectator argues that Iowa’s current primary and caucus system protects parties’ associational rights and that proposed changes such as a “top two” or approval voting system would undermine political parties and blur candidate affiliation.
This piece from the [Center for Democracy & Civic Engagement (CDCE)](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=bf5b76da59&e=c16f400a56) explores the challenge of tracking which eligible Americans cannot easily access required identity or residency documents, such as birth certificates or government-issued IDs. It claims that proposed rule changes requiring documentary proof of citizenship for voter registration would risk imposing serious barriers on these individuals.
In a [recent episode](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=af035e8a41&e=c16f400a56) of the American Enterprise Institute’s The Voting Booth, co-hosts John Fortier and Don Palmer are joined by Justin Riemer, President and CEO of USGI and Restoring Integrity and Trust in Elections (RITE) to discuss timely voting topics including litigation.
America First Policy Institute recently held a [roundtable](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=94346c9556&e=c16f400a56) on election security.
An essay published by Derek Muller in the NYU Law Democracy Project titled “[America’s Recount Addiction](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=34695e2c2f&e=c16f400a56)” argues that sweeping recount laws are overused, rarely change election outcomes, and impose heavy costs while delaying results and dampening public trust. Muller recommends that states limit automatic or candidate-requested recounts to extremely narrow margins (e.g., 0.1 % or 10 votes), make them automatic instead of candidate-driven, and require them to follow the same counting method used initially.

---
|
---
| | The Virginia State Board of Elections has scheduled a Board Meeting for [December 1](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=eb12bae492&e=c16f400a56) when it will certify the recent election results and discuss general business of the board.
