| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2929afd0-3268-4201-b4bf-3d0afa1ab2ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f48dfa1f-112a-4ae6-ae67-c646edfd95f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_**By David Shuster**_
Let it be recorded, in the annals of MAGA insanity, that twelve good citizens of Washington, D.C. just did what few men and women of sense are ever permitted to do in these times: they laughed the Trump government out of court.
Their instrument of deliverance? A sandwich.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/12a120b8-1950-4029-99b1-b14f0b08216e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/92bcf9a2-84c2-4897-b245-238d8acdc7c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Not a bomb, not a manifesto, not even a well-aimed shoe -- but a sandwich, lobbed in mild disgust at one of those agents of Immigration and Customs Enforcement who serve as the Praetorian Guard of Trump's bureaucratic power. Trump's Attorney General Pam Bondi, ever alert to the dangers of bread and luncheon meat, decided the sandwich toss was an act of war upon the state itself. So, Bondi's D.C. prosecutors hauled poor Sean Dunn -- a former Justice Department paralegal turned late night snack insurgent -- before the bar of justice, as if he had fired upon Fort Sumter.
Here, in microcosm, was the farce of the Trump age: DOJ prosecutors so puffed up with the Administration's own importance that they mistook satire for sedition.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2f0f374e-ae06-4670-9195-95e2f6dfad0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The fact this prosecution went forward at all tells us more about the administration's character than any of its lofty pronouncements about "law and order." The same Trump crowd that turns blind eyes to real abuses -- corruption, perjury, the daily vandalism of reason -- will leap from its golden MAGA chaise lounge the moment a protester hurls a hoagie. To these MAGA lunatics, a citizen's right to dissent is conditional upon silence and servility. Toss a subway sandwich, and you've struck at the very fabric of the republic!
Fortunately, the jury was having none of it. In their wisdom, or perhaps their ordinary human amusement, they found that a sandwich striking an armored agent produces no "bodily injury" except to the delicate dignity of officialdom.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/acbd2be5-3ed5-4887-b244-b81645d53381?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
Do you love sandwiches? Subscribe for free!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.0drpE9J7l382MWO1CJLnBssSJS4NSCmGpoQ_j2PNQzc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=178235374)
* * *
Relatedly, Sean Dunn's defense attorney **Julia Gatto** never contested that her client had indeed tossed the sandwich, [starting](https://substack.com/redirect/10e60659-7a76-4662-adab-e08ec3eee84d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) her opening statement with, "He did it. He threw the sandwich." But she noted the legal issue was simple. "Bodily injury. That's the standard."
Assistant US Attorney**John Parron** argued that the case was "about the fact that you can't go around throwing stuff at people when you're mad," and urged the jury to focus on that and not whatever opinions they may have about "the federal law enforcement presence in D.C." Yes, the prosecutor was pleading for justice as though the Republic itself had been assaulted in Lafayette square.
Never has a jury so heroically resisted the siren call of idiocy. They sent the Trump DOJ prosecutors back to their desks, humiliated and defeated.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2b85b3cd-f053-4328-8c86-c277fd9e905e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The testimony from **Greg Lairmore** , the agent who was struck by the sandwich, was likely persuasive for the jury, as he admitted he was not injured and had received [gag gifts](https://substack.com/redirect/10e60659-7a76-4662-adab-e08ec3eee84d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from his coworkers, including a "felony footlong" patch and a plush toy sandwich. Lairmore chuckled as he testified about these gifts and said he put the toy sandwich "on top of my shelf in the office" and the patch on his lunchbox.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/92bcf9a2-84c2-4897-b245-238d8acdc7c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ffd95bb9-6a12-4e74-b335-cea111f39544?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Lairmore had claimed that the sandwich "exploded all over my chest" and "smelled of onions and mustard," but was challenged about that on cross-examination by **Sabrina Shroff** , another of Dunn's defense attorneys.
Shroff showed Lairmore a photo of the sandwich, emphasizing how it was still wrapped up in the Subway paper, lying on the ground, and asked him if he could tell what kind of sandwich it was and why there were no evidentiary photos of the stains he claimed had been left on his uniform.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f99c5492-b5a3-4805-b162-4483f0e126ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a96db8b1-a310-47a1-baac-2a152905970c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
One imagines an O-J trial moment of sorts. "If the mustard doesn't sit, you must acquit. " Sadly, the defense attorneys didn't go there. They didn't need to.
This case should have never been brought -- and would have never been brought -- if anyone in the Trump Justice department retained the faintest whiff of proportion or common sense. Sadly, the machinery of federal government stupidity under Trump is hard to stop.
Also, this prosecution was clearly not about public safety or decorum. It was about punishing mockery, that most intolerable of crimes to Trump's stooges and their rotting, authoritarian leader.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8ee5da5c-c9a4-4acd-a93d-90fd9a71b2c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
Lunchmeat projectile free! (Usually) Subscribe!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.0drpE9J7l382MWO1CJLnBssSJS4NSCmGpoQ_j2PNQzc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=178235374)
* * *
But the jury, God bless them, understood something the Trump goons never will: liberty depends not on reverence, but on ridicule. When a government grows too absurd to laugh at, it grows too powerful to live under.
So here's to the jury -- a dozen ordinary citizens of Washington, D.C. who recognized that the true obscenity in this case was not sandwich thrower, but a Trumpian leadership so fragile that it must prosecute one.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e77f99d6-d747-4ece-89ac-40c4ca2a832f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The jury, in acquitting Sean Dunn, has done more for freedom than it may have realized.
And here's to the Subway sandwich, too -- the humble projectile that brought down not an ICE agent, but a Trumpian pretense.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/92bcf9a2-84c2-4897-b245-238d8acdc7c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a96db8b1-a310-47a1-baac-2a152905970c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

But she noted the legal issue was simple. "Bodily injury. That's the standard." Assistant US AttorneyJohn Parron argued that the case was "about the fact that you can't go around throwing stuff at people when you're mad," and urged the jury to focus on that and not whatever opinions they may have about "the federal law enforcement presence in D.C." Yes, the prosecutor was pleading for justice as though the Republic itself had been assaulted in Lafayette square. Never has a jury so heroically resisted the siren call of idiocy. They sent the Trump DOJ prosecutors back to their desks, humiliated and defeated. | | ---|---|--- The testimony from Greg Lairmore , the agent who was struck by the sandwich, was likely persuasive for the jury, as he admitted he was not injured and had received from his coworkers, including a "felony footlong" patch and a plush toy sandwich. Lairmore chuckled as he testified about these gifts and said he put the toy sandwich "on top of my shelf in the office" and the patch on his lunchbox. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- Lairmore had claimed that the sandwich "exploded all over my chest" and "smelled of onions and mustard," but was challenged about that on cross-examination by Sabrina Shroff , another of Dunn's defense attorneys. Shroff showed Lairmore a photo of the sandwich, emphasizing how it was still wrapped up in the Subway paper, lying on the ground, and asked him if he could tell what kind of sandwich it was and why there were no evidentiary photos of the stains he claimed had been left on his uniform. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- One imagines an O-J trial moment of sorts. "If the mustard doesn't sit, you must acquit. " Sadly, the defense attorneys didn't go there. They didn't need to. This case should have never been brought -- and would have never been brought -- if anyone in the Trump Justice department retained the faintest whiff of proportion or common sense. Sadly, the machinery of federal government stupidity under Trump is hard to stop. Also, this prosecution was clearly not about public safety or decorum. It was about punishing mockery, that most intolerable of crimes to Trump's stooges and their rotting, authoritarian leader. | | ---|---|--- Lunchmeat projectile free! (Usually) Subscribe! But the jury, God bless them, understood something the Trump goons never will: liberty depends not on reverence, but on ridicule. When a government grows too absurd to laugh at, it grows too powerful to live under. So here's to the jury -- a dozen ordinary citizens of Washington, D.C. who recognized that the true obscenity in this case was not sandwich thrower, but a Trumpian leadership so fragile that it must prosecute one. | | ---|---|--- The jury, in acquitting Sean Dunn, has done more for freedom than it may have realized. And here's to the Subway sandwich, too -- the humble projectile that brought down not an ICE agent, but a Trumpian pretense. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Cliff Schecter PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208 ? ?

body:
You might think any one small act can’t have that much of an impact against somebody like Trump — but that couldn’t be further from the truth.
#
Peter, after a year like this one, it’s good to celebrate your wins.
And we’ll be straight up: **These are your wins.**
We were able to make historic investments in Virginia, New Jersey, and California because supporters like you stepped up to the plate.
You might think any one small act can’t have that much of an impact against somebody like Trump — but that couldn’t be further from the truth.
[**We just put out our latest video, “Majority Maker,” and we’d be honored if you gave it a watch.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-nov25-majority-makers.RXalYi&refcode=em251107-1-120&t=1&akid=34753%2E3013755%2EpW6JQW)

And, if you’re not done yet, consider making a donation after you finish. You know exactly what it’ll go towards: **Kicking Trump’s ass at the ballot box.**
Again, thank you so much for everything you do.
It means a lot to all of us.
The team at VoteVets

Peter, after a year like this one, it’s good to celebrate your wins. And we’ll be straight up: These are your wins. We were able to make historic investments in Virginia, New Jersey, and California because supporters like you stepped up to the plate. You might think any one small act can’t have that much of an impact against somebody like Trump — but that couldn’t be further from the truth. And, if you’re not done yet, consider making a donation after you finish. You know exactly what it’ll go towards: Kicking Trump’s ass at the ballot box. Again, thank you so much for everything you do. It means a lot to all of us. name: JamesTalarico.com
subject: BREAKING FROM ROLL CALL: Cornyn is the 3rd most vulnerable senator
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Breaking news, Peter: Roll Call just released their list of the most vulnerable U.S. Senators in 2026, and John Cornyn is number three.
[**Rush a donation to James Talarico’s campaign to help us take back Texas’ U.S. Senate seat for the people.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251107-1-ak&t=1&akid=214%2E1347274%2E6-DlsH)
[DONATE](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251107-1-ak&t=2&akid=214%2E1347274%2E6-DlsH)
This confirms what we’ve known since James launched this campaign. Texas is in play.
People are fed up with the corruption and status quo in Washington. Tuesday proved that folks are ready for a new generation of leaders who are willing to stand up to billionaire mega-donors and their puppet politicians.
Roll Call highlighted that James outraised the entire field, but made clear that while Cornyn is highly vulnerable, he won’t be easy to beat: “Cornyn has Senate GOP leaders behind him, and a pro-Cornyn super PAC is already running ads supporting his campaign.”
The Big Money interests that profit from our rigged system will do whatever it takes to keep Cornyn in his seat — but we know that James can win with the power of the people behind us.
[**So please, if you can, make a contribution of any amount to help us take back Texas and send James to the U.S. Senate. Texas is likely to decide control of the Senate in 2026.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251107-1-ak&t=3&akid=214%2E1347274%2E6-DlsH)
[DONATE](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251107-1-ak&t=4&akid=214%2E1347274%2E6-DlsH)
Team Talarico

Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761

[**Check out our online store!**](https://store.jamestalarico.com?utm_source=em_footer&utm_campaign=em_footer) This is a grassroots movement to take back Texas for working people. [**Please consider making a contribution to support our fight today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jt-em-launch?refcode=em_footer&t=6&refcode2=214_1347274_6-DlsH&akid=214%2E1347274%2E6-DlsH)

And my friend Charlie Kirk made the ultimate sacrifice. It won't be in vain. In fact, Charlie's movement will only grow stronger. | | TURNING POINT ACTION, INC. # Friday, November 7, 2025

**Good Afternoon! **This week, we're taking a page from a Kentucky classroom that turned kindness into craftsmanship. When a teacher noticed one of his students was born without a hand, he didn't just offer encouragement--he [fired up his school's 3D printer](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGu2zAQRE8jdSuIFGV-FSrSuPtpUwor7VJkTJMGuTbh2wdOjpBuMMVg3vv1vX1jiCGdP_HOqzvdJi5viVuNLMJlay38S_WOZzgw7lhSY6JNj3oGpWC0G2GI7830tDpmzVPPq7IXPZtluiy9X3frlGVF0052NvOykJm-GCetJjUv5qsP62dNqdFqNWpjh2PcmdDRbhxb57gzI1OQXIbfOSTx7OI7v7gMR773cfUij9pNPzp97fS1tTacOdMHI7G0XG5DLmenrzdO8jxubxDGw3OB7EA8w5uxwFEYhQkQHiVX8SzhAI-JwOUCCFWexEngWUM6oR4-51hhIniUkOTTRdw7fe3LSo8WYgz1YjsznncM8e9R-W_Dr1X_CQAA__8iHZaT) and, with corn-based plastic, fishing line, and a lot of heart, built a functional prosthetic hand for a 10-year-old boy. On today's [_Feel Good Flyover Podcast_](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkM1u6yAUhJ8GdrH4i0kWLK5yFbWVUnXVLhGGg30qbFKbGPntK7eP0NWMZqRv8X3c7M1hwql_dSOY2EdbYrYT1CVBKTDbWvG3LaPr0bvUuXmqEIIVTBwPnB-YtsFh2qyiwUQAAZKC4boVR3WW7ZkOpvNMq6C43COKE3NKtFyeAIJTmp0omp3GOdOCM6F041kHwcXQqQg6RiCKQcCS5-Yz41QGiGnLK8yNzyNNZijlvhD5j4grEddaa7PlR3l0sP_74oofiLyuRP5_-7q8-6f2sr4809mEe8WUcGk1UawfHaYfZPmzi9WI7wAAAP__o-509Q), host Amy Hess shares an exclusive interview with the young man whose project to honor veterans with American flags and red carnations he began when he was just 10 years old. A decade later, his mission has spread nationwide, proving that a single act of gratitude can have a profound impact. [Watch it here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L9qwzAYBPCnkbcY_YuEBw0lIbSFlE7tKD5Jn2wV2U5txSJvX9w-QrfjDn7DfV7tFVJOU_8GI5rYR1vibCesa8ZScLG1pr-0jtAnD9nBMlUMwXLKjwfGDlTbACk_rGyCiYgcRYOGacWPshOqawajGUDspDp6RkFLGbxTzAFzVEhFOWuS2TXGqOaMcqlbTx0GiMHJiDpGJJJiSGVe2q85TWXAmB_zhkvr57HJZijlthLxRPiF8EuttX3M93J3uO97A8UPRFw2Is7v36cP_6xO2-sL4aoQce7k2iwm3GrKOa1KE0n7EVL-xcu_X9kM_wkAAP__98Z3jg). ## WWI Letters Wash Ashore in Australia
Two letters written by Australian soldiers on their way to fight in World War I [have washed up](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAMQL8GNk5JMHAZMnS57bp2jEzigKtATiG96P6-ou0fdHvWkyw_f9ztHTnyvrzjRiYswZaQ7E71iFQKZVsr_9Kx4cIO44x5r-S9VUINnZSdmKxHji8LrTeBSFHfkpHTqAbQ_ajb1QwBJh2QcA5a99pJdCCd6OUVSCLpls25TUoxKSkUTBcnZvIY_AyBphCoAUGeS8qXz8R7WSnEV3pSvri0tdGspTyOpn9r1K1RN3ycAac6h1zYRTrp6ygZI2P3V9TNqZRI3Uzo1q5W7o4UPZ9mGASAvjoHw-i8VsKN2EtAAg9Batdm4x-VY-RjnBoQy4Ycf44p__7i06jvAAAA__9Eq4mI) more than a century later on a remote beach in Western Australia. [The notes](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO8yAQhJ_G7rCA4GAXFH-TLn97JVqzi70nbCLDBeXtT7l7hOumGM3o-z7u_g6c-Fj_w04urtHXmP1BrSSqlU7fGv-mssPKAdIC59EI0WupR6GUkNYjcHp506OLRJouPTllr3o08-U695uDSaGdSRkV1SxDmNQS9YhoQQZr5dSze68pJa1WUhs7BLkQQsTFRLIxUmckIdd8Dp-Zj7pRTK_8pHMIee-T22p9lO7yr9O3Tt8CDG-C4QVbzu9Gp2-tsSg5IdNZxE6lwEpFLLnWRCLmrwOFlmqSerTzsNU99afDR-OUuFxtZ-S6A6efu_pnT0-nvwMAAP__q_F_vg), dated Aug. 15, 1916, were tucked inside a Schweppes bottle and written just days after Privates Malcolm Neville and William Harley left Adelaide for France. Malcolm, who later died in battle, wrote warmly to his mother back home. William, who survived the war but suffered from gas exposure, offered well wishes to whoever might find the bottle. Their families called the discovery emotional and miraculous--a final message from the past, finally delivered. [Watch a video report](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDHO4yAUhE9jd1iAjbELim3SZdstrYd52G-FIQISlNuvkj3C3430SaOZ7899uwMFisdvuND4w2_Vpy1iKwFrxby1Rv9TueCgHYKFHBs6t0kuFROCcb05oPDept4Zjyhx7NEIPUs1reO89qdRUniPs1inVSyAi3KzVUp7P2sLblx6Mp82IbiWgstJDzu36MA7O3nU3mM3cXRUUx7-Jor1RB_e6YV52NPVB3PW-ijd-KuTt07eWmtDtPvnxYd38vYih-lLiJUUHGEu7MJS4MDCKDJgNtUakPn0jI6lyOBZaoZAEJlF2E8mFV_5KtW4yLnPxj0ahUBl1t3EjwsofLfUH0t8GfkvAAD__-Usie0).
## School Throws Surprise Wedding for Principal
Students and staff at Buffalo Academy of Science in New York [surprised their principal](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO5CAURE-DM1uAaWMHBJs46003RBg-9l9hsIBp1HP6kWeOMFkFpSq99--pnwYDxv2vOUH53evqk47QSoBaIevW8CeV0-xoTdhMjg2c05zyR89YT6V2BsNbi84pD8Bh7EAxOfGHWMZp6Q4lrZy9pzOTQMdFzly6ad6csMsmHszNHap7jTEqOaNcyMHSDZzxbhMepPdABAWHNeXhf8JYD_DhnV6QB5vOLqij1quQ8Q_hK-Fra21o6YT4eZr9bhC-hmRN6G8wwtcrY7R4mdCXj3xlLOD60gBqf5Nh3Ptij5QC4WuXlbsahoBlkkTQ_TQYvl_rr3W9FP8KAAD__7s_g68) with a heartfelt ceremony just days before her real wedding. After [Principal Brianna Lanoye](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO3DAMRL_G7mjYsrzeK1yk2e7SpjRokbKZ00oLiVnBfx_48gnpCM5gMG9-fa6fKEHi_hOfvPjdr-rTGrmWwKqc11rl31WeuIvDsGGOlYlW05sJhgH6eSWUcK62pcUzGx5bXob5Zib7Md4-2mO5O-t7S3dvnbvZ-zZMhI62cZjMNk53amW50oahn83QGzt3rt-Y0NNmPc_ec2N7JtGUu99Joh7sw5nenDuXnm1YDtVXacYfjXk05lFr7faU6MKIrDXlry7lvTEPiUUxKihjLuBThleW6OSFAeqRoGJUJuA35xOK_iGOCqhwcIYLWuIOJYEefELF8PUtUKrxegFKCXzZiztSCo15tHmhV5UQpNzmxvb7EyV8d9b_Hvu9mL8BAAD__xz4m98) joked about inviting the whole school to her big day, teachers and students made it happen--complete with a makeshift gown, candy Ring Pops, and a student officiant. More than 400 students packed the courtyard to watch Lanoye and fiance Zach Klapp exchange playful vows. Brianna called the moment "unforgettable" and said she was "blown away" by the love. [Watch video report](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0Dtu6zAUBNDVUJ0F_kxaBQs_GH5NnNbpCH7ulRhQoiHRJpzVB06WkG4wA5xirhd7cSmnZXx3Mxgc0VYsdoG2ZagVVtta-k3b7MYUXPZuXRrEaDnl-x1jO6ptdCk_reyiQQAOogPDtOJ7OQg1dJMZqJcDBh019xhA4SEehBYqHjxVSvAumZfGGNWcUS51H6iH6DB6iaARgUgKMdWy9p8lLXUCzM_ygLUPZe6ymWq9bUQcCT8Tfm6t9c9yr3cPr_3VuBomIs4PIk7_r6evt_1HU_-O3WriraWc06Y0kXScXco_ZP3zFw_DvwMAAP__EQV1VA).
## Senior Living Director Moves In
Jennifer Tapner, 52, [lives alongside](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuhDAQBNCvMR3IXgyGwkUaukubEhm8ho0WfMLWWfx9dMknpHvSSDPSfD3mhyOmc_t0B9qwhTmHOJ9YEmPOeM2l0J_S4TZaHS_uOgt6P4OErlaqlmb2jviedeVtQARsK7TK9NDpse3HarfQtmsnB7-MvsV-0Mqg0VqPK6qhV4upyL7blJIGlARtmlUu6F3wiw5oQkChJXrK8Wq-I515x8B3fOHVrPGo2O45P5NoPwRMAqZSSnO7PcZ3KmBiCpjyzShgclemlTEJmOioO6iZXlgnPCleb9O51WrQIJXpZbPng6vL-mchZkq9EVpuhyP-3c3_Puxl4ScAAP__TOOBnA) the 200 residents she manages at The Watermark at Brooklyn Heights, a senior living community in New York. After years in a national leadership role, she returned to direct care to reconnect with the people she serves--sharing meals, walking dogs, and training for a marathon with residents cheering her on. [Living on-site](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAUhJ_G7rDgBJu4oLhNutx2SwTmYJ8VP5Fhg_L2q908wnZTjGb0fR93c7cUKe__bUId9mBaKCZjrxFbw9P0Tu9Uk91ps9HZM3f03gCHmQnBuDLeUnwZOXodEAEvI2qhFpjlelnW8dB-XYJ0wkm8AnhQV3DbPOO6SA9BwDaS_lkTgisQHKSaNu7Q2-CdDKhCwEFy9NTKOX0Wyu3AEF_liee0lTRGfbT2qMPl3wC3AW6998l9VcpYK-VK_t0b4JZstjuyipnKyY6SkKXypLwzyqzbytqBzGFtzONGlUpmb0w-nto_OsVIdVGD5HuyFH_f25-1PTV8BwAA___lvIXi) has deepened her empathy and helped her better understand residents' everyday concerns and emotional needs. Jennifer says the experience has made her a better listener and advocate, seeing firsthand the importance of treating older adults as individuals, not statistics.

clean_body: Two letters written by Australian soldiers on their way to fight in World War I more than a century later on a remote beach in Western Australia. | | | | | | Friday, November 7, 2025 ---|--- Good Afternoon! This week, we're taking a page from a Kentucky classroom that turned kindness into craftsmanship. When a teacher noticed one of his students was born without a hand, he didn't just offer encouragement--he and, with corn-based plastic, fishing line, and a lot of heart, built a functional prosthetic hand for a 10-year-old boy. On today's , host Amy Hess shares an exclusive interview with the young man whose project to honor veterans with American flags and red carnations he began when he was just 10 years old. A decade later, his mission has spread nationwide, proving that a single act of gratitude can have a profound impact. . As inflation surges and economic warning signs flash red, now's the time to follow this wealth-protection strategy from today's sponsor, . | WWI Letters Wash Ashore in Australia Two letters written by Australian soldiers on their way to fight in World War I more than a century later on a remote beach in Western Australia. , dated Aug. 15, 1916, were tucked inside a Schweppes bottle and written just days after Privates Malcolm Neville and William Harley left Adelaide for France. Malcolm, who later died in battle, wrote warmly to his mother back home. William, who survived the war but suffered from gas exposure, offered well wishes to whoever might find the bottle. Their families called the discovery emotional and miraculous--a final message from the past, finally delivered. . School Throws Surprise Wedding for Principal Students and staff at Buffalo Academy of Science in New York with a heartfelt ceremony just days before her real wedding. After joked about inviting the whole school to her big day, teachers and students made it happen--complete with a makeshift gown, candy Ring Pops, and a student officiant. More than 400 students packed the courtyard to watch Lanoye and fiance Zach Klapp exchange playful vows. Brianna called the moment "unforgettable" and said she was "blown away" by the love. . Senior Living Director Moves In Jennifer Tapner, 52, the 200 residents she manages at The Watermark at Brooklyn Heights, a senior living community in New York. After years in a national leadership role, she returned to direct care to reconnect with the people she serves--sharing meals, walking dogs, and training for a marathon with residents cheering her on. has deepened her empathy and helped her better understand residents' everyday concerns and emotional needs. Jennifer says the experience has made her a better listener and advocate, seeing firsthand the importance of treating older adults as individuals, not statistics. | The following story is featured exclusively on The Feel Good Flyover Podcast.Clicking the link will take you directly to this story: ➤ Amy Hess shares an exclusive interview with a California man who has spent the last decade honoring veterans, a mission he began at just 10 years old. name: John Fetterman
subject: you protected our democracy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
[ https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51612?t=1001&akid=10415%2E434096%2EuxZW44 ]John Fetterman

Hey Derek,

John here – and I just had to say this straight: What you did Tuesday was monumental.

Because of you, our three Supreme Court justices were retained. That’s a d*mn big deal. It means we’ve still got strong, steady voices protecting fair elections, reproductive freedom, LGBTQIA+ rights, and clean air and water for every Pennsylvanian. 💪

Here’s the truth – state supreme courts are where the rubber meets the road. When national politics turns into a circus, these justices keep things fair and grounded. Remember 2020? Our court stood tall against Trump’s lies about election fraud and kept our democracy intact.

They’ve drawn fairer maps. They’ve defended people’s rights. They’ve kept the guardrails on when politicians tried to break them.

So yeah, this win matters – a lot. You showed up. You made your voice heard. And you reminded the country that Pennsylvanians don’t back down from a fight for justice.

If you're fired up like I am and ready to help elect more steady leaders in next year's midterm elections, you can pitch in $5, $25, or whatever you can afford right now – because wins like this don't just happen out of thin air.

https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52302?t=1002&akid=10415%2E434096%2EuxZW44

Thank you for voting like democracy depends on it – because it does.

John ❤️

--------

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here:

Paid for by Fetterman for PA.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.johnfetterman.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1003&akid=10415%2E434096%2EuxZW44

name: Kim Schrier
subject: April Fools, right?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
November marks American Diabetes Month—a time for us to learn about the disease, how to identify it early, and how to manage it. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118873717/581173555/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNWNlMWViNS0xZGJjLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xuz9qiRu_D6gJyKT1EuWhpdf_ufa-0EknIKxRz7f-jU=&emci=7f0186eb-f1bb-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=f5ce1eb5-1dbc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter,

Forty years ago this year, on April Fool’s Day, my doctor told me I had Type 1 Diabetes.

_Yes, I asked him if it was a joke. No, it wasn’t one._

It was scary for me because I’d have to take shots and prick my fingers to test my blood sugar. It was scary for my parents because at that time, people with diabetes didn’t always stay healthy, and they worried about what having diabetes would mean for the rest of my life.

Thankfully, I had a doctor, educators, and incredible parents who supported me and helped me navigate this new normal and take the best possible care of myself. My dad, in 1985, would plot my blood sugars on our new Macintosh computer to analyze trends.

Dr. Kaufman was my lifesaver at the time. She also inspired me to become a pediatrician. I’m so grateful I had the care I did as a teenager because it got me off to the right start, and I want to make sure everyone with diabetes gets the care they deserve.

**November marks American Diabetes Month—a time for us to learn about the disease, how to identify it early, and how to manage it. With prompt detection and good management, people with diabetes now live very healthy lives.**

Education about managing this disease is crucial and saves lives. That’s why I’ve used my role in Congress to introduce the Expanding Access to Diabetes Self-Management Training Act, a bill that would strengthen diabetes education and care for seniors.

I was proud to cap the cost of insulin at $35 per month for people on Medicare, and was excited afterwards to see that insulin manufacturers voluntarily dropped the prices on many types of insulin, finally making life-saving insulin affordable for everyone.

I’ve introduced bipartisan and bicameral legislation to expand screening for Type 1 Diabetes, and have supported legislation that would help people with pre-diabetes avoid developing Type 2 Diabetes.

And, of course, I always advocate for the most robust NIH funding to support this nation’s traditionally cutting-edge research. This includes diabetes research.

I’m proud of the work I’ve done to support others with this disease, but I know we still have a long way to go to ensure everyone living with diabetes has the knowledge, care, and support they need to live long and healthy lives. Everyone deserves access to affordable care and medications.

[**We need robust research and a healthcare system that works for all of us. We need stronger coverage, affordable medicines and premiums, lower deductibles and co-pays, and less red tape. This American Diabetes Month, I pledge to continue my work to strengthen our access to healthcare, but I need you with me. Will you add your name next to mine to demand that Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans reverse their cuts to Medicaid and the ACA, and that Congress continue the fight for better healthcare access for all? >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118873718/581173556/-915068942?ms=em25_251107_diabet_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&refcode=em25_251107_diabet_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&source=em25_251107_diabet_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251107_diabet_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=PJAsj7GJxR3XfHO+rPd58nXCStOZ2i0J9YopF8EHHWruzUbBlFugfS/MuDf6vU2pUtllH6RwKPpKgbSgyq/8LX0F5ejay9moPyOogPbrNVp5TC1ZNg9Q5+a9zVJGIBgqJhYRXWoefnJApeCqd+d0O5F90isH2syNCt8W2ugKRQw=&refcode2=f5ce1eb5-1dbc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNWNlMWViNS0xZGJjLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xuz9qiRu_D6gJyKT1EuWhpdf_ufa-0EknIKxRz7f-jU=&emci=7f0186eb-f1bb-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=f5ce1eb5-1dbc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=15820338) [ADD YOUR NAME →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118873719/581173557/-915068942?ms=em25_251107_diabet_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&refcode=em25_251107_diabet_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&source=em25_251107_diabet_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251107_diabet_engg_gq_act___dks_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=PJAsj7GJxR3XfHO+rPd58nXCStOZ2i0J9YopF8EHHWruzUbBlFugfS/MuDf6vU2pUtllH6RwKPpKgbSgyq/8LX0F5ejay9moPyOogPbrNVp5TC1ZNg9Q5+a9zVJGIBgqJhYRXWoefnJApeCqd+d0O5F90isH2syNCt8W2ugKRQw=&refcode2=f5ce1eb5-1dbc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNWNlMWViNS0xZGJjLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xuz9qiRu_D6gJyKT1EuWhpdf_ufa-0EknIKxRz7f-jU=&emci=7f0186eb-f1bb-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&emdi=f5ce1eb5-1dbc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&ceid=15820338) Thank you for taking the time to read my story,

name: Rusty Hicks
subject: A great win, and now on to 2026
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5]
Dear friend,
We did it.
Because of you — your energy, your organizing, your votes — California made history this week and passed Proposition 50.
You stood up against Donald Trump’s election-rigging scheme and proved that when democracy is under attack, Californians fight back. From this campaign suddenly emerging in August to the final ballot returned, our grassroots movement showed the country what people-powered democracy looks like.
This victory wasn’t just about California. It was about protecting every voter’s voice and defending our democracy across all 50 states.
But our work doesn’t stop here.
Now that we have passed Prop 50, we begin our next mission together: To flip the U.S. House and restore Democratic control in Washington.
Passing Prop 50 was never going to be the finish line. It is the starting point — a moment that launches our mission to take back the House of Representatives and restore balance and decency in Washington.
Now that Californians have put voters, not MAGA politicians, in the driver’s seat of our democracy, we now look to 2026. We must channel the same energy, commitment, and unity that fueled this campaign into organizing to win back the U.S. House Majority. Every conversation, every dollar, and every new volunteer will help us flip the seats we need to secure a Democratic majority and protect the progress we’ve made.
So as we celebrate this incredible win for democracy, I have one simple request: Stay with us. Let’s carry this momentum forward and build the organizing power we’ll need to take back the House. If you are ready to renew your support with a donation toward our swing seat campaigns, you can do so today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-nov25-redistricting?&refcode=&amount=]

Yours, very sincerely,
Rusty Hicks
Chair
California Democratic Party

name: Seth for Massachusetts
subject: October was BIG month for our team
body:
Here's what we accomplished, friend →

| |
---
Friend, The government has been shut down, but that hasn't stopped Seth from fighting for working families. In fact, between Seth's work in Washington and the launch of our Senate campaign, we've been busier than ever. Take a look: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118873999/581174591/2090747700?refcode=251107_s4m_OctRecap_img&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjlkMmNhYy0xZWJjLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dWADjb4MPjtZgwoBmgA1BDGvKhpTl5FEvzJpf5yDlvo=) Thanks so much for supporting that work, team. We're honored to have you with us. **[CHIP IN](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118874000/581174592/1375639745?refcode=251107_s4m_OctRecap_b1&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjlkMmNhYy0xZWJjLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dWADjb4MPjtZgwoBmgA1BDGvKhpTl5FEvzJpf5yDlvo=)** — Seth for Massachusetts
| | |
---|---|---

### About Seth Moulton
Seth Moulton is a father, a husband, a Marine, and a Democrat with a mission to bring a new generation of leaders to Congress. He is the proud father of two girls, Emmy and Caroline, and husband to his wife, Liz. name: Mark Kelly
subject: I owe you an explanation
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
I often get asked what I’m doing, right now, to counter Trump.
#

Folks, Mark Kelly here.
I often get asked what I’m doing right now to counter Trump.
I think I owe it to you to explain what exactly it is that I’m doing, and why I’m doing it.
First off, I’m talking to Republicans. I know that might not sound all that impressive, but with tensions as high as they are, it’s getting kind of rare. I’m trying to continue to set an example and show you can still find common ground with people you disagree with.
What we do in response makes all the difference. I firmly believe that the only way we’ll see ourselves out of the situation we are in now, the only way we can bring back some real accountability, is by retaking majorities in the House and Senate. And that’s exactly what I’ve been working towards.
In Arizona, I’m working to support the Arizona Democratic Party and our slate of candidates. I’m making sure the people running — from the top of the ballot to the bottom — are getting the help they need from me. And I continue to help build out the infrastructure it’s going to take to win across the state next year.
And around the country, I’m doing the same thing. In the Senate, we need to defend all of our seats and grab a few more to win a majority. I took the Vice Chair position at the DSCC because I knew I could make a difference by helping out the candidates running in those races.
I’m not doing any of this for attention, publicity, or a thank you. I’m doing it because it’s what I can do to turn things around, and because we have to win next year.
Now, if any of that sounds like something you’d like to be a part of, I’ve got a humble request for you:
[**This work does not happen without the support of people like you. So, I’m asking you to chip in today. Can I count on you?**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-nov25/.mVeyuq&refcode=em251107-1-700k&t=1&akid=6239%2E6688874%2EJQpQ4f)

| | **[Donate $3](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1396?c0=mek-em-nov25/.HrSi9M&refcode=em251107-1-700k&t=20&akid=6239%2E6688874%2EJQpQ4f)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1397?c0=mek-em-nov25/.LFB52X&refcode=em251107-1-700k&t=21&akid=6239%2E6688874%2EJQpQ4f)**
---

| | **[Donate $50](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1390?c0=mek-em-nov25/.AgEJlg&refcode=em251107-1-700k&t=22&akid=6239%2E6688874%2EJQpQ4f)**
---

---
****
Thank you in advance for the help.
It means a lot to me.
Mark

name: David Hogg
subject: Nancy Pelosi
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Here’s what this means for our movement.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251107-1&t=1&akid=2815%2E587540%2E0Ri1rS)
Big news, Peter:
**Former Speaker of the House, Nancy Pelosi, just announced her retirement** after 40+ years of representing San Francisco in Congress.
Before I tell you a quick story, I want to say this to Speaker Pelosi:
**Thank you.**
Thank you for passing the Affordable Care Act, for delivering the first major federal gun violence prevention legislation in three decades after the Uvalde and Buffalo massacres, for securing life-saving research for HIV/AIDS, and for standing up to Trump.
This will be a longer email than usual which my fundraising team hates because people usually only donate if you ask them in the beginning of the email. But stay with me, because this is important.
[](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251107_1_pelosi.vE78WV&refcode=em20251107-2815&amount=5&t=3&akid=2815%2E587540%2E0Ri1rS)
With Speaker Pelosi after the passage of the first gun law in 30 years.
Now, here’s a story about me and Speaker Pelosi I’ve never told before—
After Parkland, my classmates and I were devastated and had an extreme distrust of politicians and adults who we felt were responsible for the weak gun laws that enabled the 19-year-old shooter who killed our administrators and classmates, to be able to own an AR-15, despite repeatedly threatening to shoot up our high school.
Naturally, Nancy Pelosi was one of those people we were cautious about trusting. But despite that, she continued to show up for us. At the March for Our Lives in DC in 2018, we were extremely strict about not having any politicians speak. Speaker Pelosi still showed up just to protest with us (in sun glasses and a hat that she took off at one point with the students and said, “Hey guys, it’s Nancy Pelosi” showing us how she was still with us). But she didn’t just stop there.
When we sent a 16-year-old co-founder of March For Our Lives up to DC to be a lobbyist for us, Speaker Pelosi and her team personally mentored him and showed him the ropes of Congress. The co-founder became so successful that even after he left lobbying to go to college, I would still get questions about him years later from Members of Congress.
Then in 2022, after the Uvalde and Buffalo shootings when March For Our Lives wanted to do its second March on the National Mall in front of the White House, we needed a permit that usually takes months to get approved. We only had about a week to get it approved. I saw Speaker Pelosi at a protest and asked her to help us get the permit done. And once again, she showed up and cut through the red tape, making the second March possible in DC. (Secretary Deb Haaland was also a massive help in that. So thank you, Sec. Haaland! I know you get our emails!). That March, in combination with pressure from our allies in the gun reform movement, resulted in the first federal gun law in 30 years being passed. It’s now been used well over 1,000 times to stop high risk individuals under 21 from buying guns like the AR-15 and since its passage there's been about a 20% drop in gun homicides.
What has impressed me most about Speaker Pelosi is that even at times when I have not agreed with her, or when I’ve called her out publicly (saying things like, “F... Democrats in Congress for screwing over the survivors of gun violence” over some extreme frustration I had over a vote on banning assault weapons years ago), and even after I once protested outside of her office — she has time and time again delivered and showed up for the survivors of gun violence.
So, Speaker Pelosi, thank you for proving me wrong and showing me that sometimes politicians actually can do the right thing. Thank you, as the first woman elected House Speaker, for delivering the votes ... _Every. Single. Time._ **And thank you, Speaker Pelosi, for making room for a new generation to lead and for helping us while in power too.**
That story of how she helped the gun safety movement is why when I announced Leaders We Deserve’s mission to elect young progressives and primary ineffective Democrats, I made something clear: **Not every older politician needs to be replaced.** In fact, I specifically said Speaker Pelosi would _not_ be a target. Why? Because she’s damn good at her job. She's a juggernaut. There’s a reason she drove Trump absolutely insane AND got legislation passed that saved millions of lives.
Our movement isn’t just about age. It’s about effectiveness, courage, and making space for new leadership that’s not afraid to fight for their communities, meet the moment, and take on Trump and MAGA with everything they’ve got.
And one thing you can never say about Nancy Pelosi? That she’s _ineffective,_ or afraid to fight Trump.
Peter, this is a huge moment for our movement. We all know how influential Speaker Pelosi is — where she goes, so goes the caucus, so goes the Democratic Party, and so goes the country. And she knows **voters are crying out for younger leadership and next-generation Democrats.**
I hope more Democrats in Congress follow her example and pass the torch, while continuing to advise the next generation — because we need an intergenerational coalition to build a stronger Democratic Party.
Now it’s on us to carry this momentum forward — to elect bold, progressive leaders who will deliver results like Speaker Pelosi and reflect a new generation.
**[If you’re with me and you’re ready to see the next generation of leaders take the reins, please make a $5 contribution now to stand with us in this fight.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251107_1_pelosi.vE78WV&refcode=em20251107-2815&amount=5&t=4&akid=2815%2E587540%2E0Ri1rS)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251107_1_pelosi.vE78WV&amount=5&refcode=em20251107-2815&t=23&akid=2815%2E587540%2E0Ri1rS)**
Thank you for your support,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20251107-1&t=24&refcode2=2815_587540_0Ri1rS&akid=2815%2E587540%2E0Ri1rS)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=25&akid=2815%2E587540%2E0Ri1rS)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=26&akid=2815%2E587540%2E0Ri1rS)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

name: Haley Stevens
subject: Does this sound familiar?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
What I'm hearing, and how you can help

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1310%2E49239%2EJqX7EN)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8122?t=2&akid=1310%2E49239%2EJqX7EN)
---
null,

If you’ve met me on the campaign trail, you know I’m not shy about asking Michiganders what’s going on in their lives. I want to understand what matters most to you – and here’s what I’ve been hearing:
🛟 Families are worried about losing Medicare and Social Security.
🩺 Health care costs are still out of control.
💰 Good-paying jobs are too hard to find – and too easy to lose.
💥 And people are scared about the future of our democracy.
If you’re fired up about any of that – and ready to elect a fighter for working families – [**will you do me a favor and add your name now to endorse our grassroots Senate campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8123?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=3&akid=1310%2E49239%2EJqX7EN)
| | [Add Your Name](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8123?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=4&akid=1310%2E49239%2EJqX7EN)
---
I’m running for Senate because I care deeply about the working people of Michigan. And I’ve spent my career delivering results by:
📌 Helping to save over 200,000 Michigan jobs on President Obama’s Auto Rescue Task Force.
📌 Championing the law to bring semiconductor manufacturing back home.
📌 Introducing the Stop Trump’s Abuse of Power Act and writing articles of impeachment against RFK Jr. to restore checks and balances.
I know there’s _a lot_ of work to do. **But I believe public service should be about showing up, listening, and getting things done** – not playing political games or helping your rich donors.

Now, with MAGA Mike Rogers backed by $5 million in dark money and national Republicans targeting this seat in Michigan, I'm asking you personally:
**[Will you join my team before midnight by adding your name? We’re still 21% short of our 2,500-signature goal – and your endorsement would mean the world to me.](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/8123?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1310%2E49239%2EJqX7EN)**

Thanks for standing with me.

With grit and gratitude,

Haley

The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. | | It has been more than a month since the government shutdown. Food assistance through SNAP ran out of money last Friday. 38,000 people in Northwest Washington can no longer afford to put food on the table to feed their families because of the Trump Administration. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118874253/581175504/1596212816?refcode=2025-11-08-snap&recurring=&amount=16&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=6afc7635-20bc-f011-8194-00224823ff9b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWZjNzYzNS0yMGJjLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDIyNDgyM2ZmOWIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pw2JMNJIbaqOaBGKzQJn5tCR1hnVwKvhW7r57flL6xY=) The Trump Administration can fully fund SNAP through this month, but they are choosing not to. This is a deliberate choice to let kids, veterans, and seniors go hungry. The Administration has allowed healthcare costs, grocery prices, and gas prices to increase. When Trump fast-tracked his Big Ugly Bill this summer, he cut billions in funding for SNAP through 2034. He refuses to fund food assistance programs because he does not care about working families. He cares about the richest Americans and the largest corporations, and this decision is another clear indication of that. I know how important SNAP benefits are. The long-term consequences of hungry families and children are profound, which is why I am working to fix healthcare, reopen the government, and push the administration to make the right choice on SNAP benefits. name: Katie Porter
subject: Here's a question for Donald Trump:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
How come he’s cutting back on commercial planes before grounding the private ones carrying his billionaire friends?
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40375%2E3422442%2Engjz05)
Peter,
Here's a question for Donald Trump as he forces airlines to cancel thousands of flights across the country:
**How come he’s cutting back on commercial planes before grounding the private ones carrying his billionaire friends?**
[
](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251107_1_ak.tJ8APt&refcode=em20251107-40375&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40375%2E3422442%2Engjz05)
What about the private jets shuttling CEOs, billionaires and the 1%? There are thousands of private jet flights in the U.S. every day—why not ground those before disrupting travel for the vast majority of Americans?
One way to free up traffic and limit delays and cancellations for most Americans is to start with those who are carrying the fewest people.
It's just today's example of who Donald Trump cares about—and it definitely isn't us. He only cares about the wealthy and well-connected.
Donald Trump cares about billionaires. I care about everyday, working people who are fighting to get by.
**[Can you chip in $5 to our campaign today to help me win so I can protect the interests of working families?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251107_1_ak.tJ8APt&refcode=em20251107-40375&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40375%2E3422442%2Engjz05)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251107_1_ak.tJ8APt&refcode=em20251107-40375&t=23&akid=40375%2E3422442%2Engjz05)
Thank you,
Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251107-40375&amounts=&t=24&refcode2=40375_3422442_ngjz05&akid=40375%2E3422442%2Engjz05)**

subject: What a win for Democrats! 🌊
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emerge America
body:
This movement is winning where it matters most.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118874515/581176371/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-11-07_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ3ODg5MmU2LTIwYmMtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ymXP5yZhukCKe-Xa4SJlqv0hYK8Tj81IMdKRtVdsOo4=)

With your support, we can train more women to run and win in 2026.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118874518/581176376/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-11-07_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ3ODg5MmU2LTIwYmMtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ymXP5yZhukCKe-Xa4SJlqv0hYK8Tj81IMdKRtVdsOo4=)
---
| [GIVE NOW ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118874521/581176380/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-11-07_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ3ODg5MmU2LTIwYmMtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMjI0ODIzZmY5YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ymXP5yZhukCKe-Xa4SJlqv0hYK8Tj81IMdKRtVdsOo4=)
---
Peter,

What a win for Democrats!

From the governor’s mansion, to state legislatures, to city halls, Emerge alums won up and down the ballot across the country.

Our biggest wins came in Virginia. Emerge alum **Abigail Spanberger became the first woman Governor in the Commonwealth** —and she’s coming into office with a Democratic trifecta, thanks in large part to Emerge alums.

Emerge had 33 women on the ballot for the Virginia House of Delegates. **25 alums won their races, giving Democrats a supermajority in the House!** This means that fully one quarter of the Virginia House of Delegates will now be represented by Emerge alums.

**All 8 of our alums running in red-to-blue House of Delegates races won** , replacing Republicans with women of the New American Majority, Black and Brown, women of color, LGBTQ+ women, young and unmarried women.

_These victories didn’t happen by chance._ They’re the result of years of trainings, mentorship, and community that make Emerge the most effective pipeline for Democratic women leaders in the nation.

**That’s why first-time Emerge candidates are six times more likely to win their races.**

_And while the numbers are still coming in, one thing is clear: this movement is winning where it matters most._

Every single one of these wins is powered by people like you who believe that training, supporting, and electing Democratic women changes the future.

clean_body: This movement is winning where it matters most. | = | | | = | = Peter, What a win for Democrats! From the governor’s mansion, to state legislatures, to city halls, Emerge alums won up and down the ballot across the country. Our biggest wins came in Virginia. Emerge alum Abigail Spanberger became the first woman Governor in the Commonwealth —and she’s coming into office with a Democratic trifecta, thanks in large part to Emerge alums. Emerge had 33 women on the ballot for the Virginia House of Delegates. 25 alums won their races, giving Democrats a supermajority in the House! This means that fully one quarter of the Virginia House of Delegates will now be represented by Emerge alums. All 8 of our alums running in red-to-blue House of Delegates races won , replacing Republicans with women of the New American Majority, Black and Brown, women of color, LGBTQ+ women, young and unmarried women. These victories didn’t happen by chance. They’re the result of years of trainings, mentorship, and community that make Emerge the most effective pipeline for Democratic women leaders in the nation. That’s why first-time Emerge candidates are six times more likely to win their races. And while the numbers are still coming in, one thing is clear: this movement is winning where it matters most. Every single one of these wins is powered by people like you who believe that training, supporting, and electing Democratic women changes the future. Now, we build on this momentum. The next class of Emerge women are already training, ready to take on what comes next. = | | | = | = | = | = | = — Team Emerge * | | = Paid for by Emerge America Emerge America 818 Connecticut Avenue NW Suite 450 Washington, DC 20006 United States | = =

name: He did WHAT??? >>> Fight For Reform
subject: it’s about Mike Johnson
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_) [**BREAKING FROM _CNN:_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=3&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_) [**”Frustrated Arizonans have waited more than a month for their new congresswoman to be seated”**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=4&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_) Listen, we’ll get straight to the point...

[**_Mike Johnson is a complete and total DISASTER!!!_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=5&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_) [ **Before you read any further, sign onto our petition ASAP to demand Congress FIRE Mike Johnson >>**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=6&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_) | [ADD MY NAME NOW →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=7&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_)
---
Listen, in the nine months since Trump has been back in office, Mike Johnson has:

**⇒ SHUT DOWN** the government.

**⇒ REFUSED** to swear in a democratically-elected Representative from Arizona, denying thousands of voters representation in Congress.

**⇒ BLOCKED** the release of the Epstein files, at the behest of Donald Trump.

So, we’re creating a nationwide petition DEMANDING Congress FIRE Mike Johnson immediately – [**but we NEED to hit 75,000 signatures before 8 AM tomorrow in order to prove there’s widespread support to remove him.**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=8&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_)
[ **Please – you have been selected as a top Democrat in your area. Will you step up and add your name to our petition today?? →→**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=9&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_)
[](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=10&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_)
[ADD MY NAME NOW →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=11&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_)
---
We know this is an ambitious goal.

_But we’re scared to think about where our country will be if Mike Johnson continues as House Speaker._

[**Please, we just need 8,031 more signatures. We’re so close!!!**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=12&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_)
[ **Please, help us meet our goal in the next 8 hours so we can take this straight to D.C. and DEMAND Congress fire Mike Johnson immediately! →**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=13&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_)
[ADD MY NAME NOW →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69837?t=14&akid=23586%2E4449941%2EGpbSI_)
---
Thanks,

Fight for Reform

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | Trump dropped 9 words that sent shockwaves through the financial world: It wasn’t a rant. It was a warning. And insiders say it’s just the beginning. His economic inner circle is already building the foundation for a hard-asset reset — while the Fed keeps printing. This isn't a theory. It's already happening. Golden Portfolio IV (GPIV) reveals the short list of gold stocks positioned to surge as Trump’s vision becomes reality. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Eugene DePasquale
subject: a true victory for PA
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party
body:
| |
---
Peter,

On Tuesday, Pennsylvanians voted to retain Justices Donohue, Dougherty, and Wecht. This is a significant win for our party and our state, as these justices will continue to stand for the rule of law, instead of bowing to the will of the Trump administration. **This is nothing short of a true victory for Pennsylvania.** These justices will work to defend our rights in the Commonwealth, defending the rule of law, safeguarding our elections, and protecting our Constitutional freedoms.

Billionaires tried to buy our courts, pouring in money to confuse voters, but Pennsylvanians saw right through it. Right here in the birthplace of democracy, we defeated the right-wing agenda and proved that Pennsylvania will always stand up for freedoms.

Thank you so much for being a part of this fight with us. Let’s carry this momentum all the way into 2026 and beyond.

— Eugene

clean_body: | | Peter, On Tuesday, Pennsylvanians voted to retain Justices Donohue, Dougherty, and Wecht. This is a significant win for our party and our state, as these justices will continue to stand for the rule of law, instead of bowing to the will of the Trump administration. This is nothing short of a true victory for Pennsylvania. These justices will work to defend our rights in the Commonwealth, defending the rule of law, safeguarding our elections, and protecting our Constitutional freedoms. Billionaires tried to buy our courts, pouring in money to confuse voters, but Pennsylvanians saw right through it. Right here in the birthplace of democracy, we defeated the right-wing agenda and proved that Pennsylvania will always stand up for freedoms. Thank you so much for being a part of this fight with us. Let’s carry this momentum all the way into 2026 and beyond. — Eugene = | Follow us on , , , and **! Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party. Friend, They shot my father. Then, they killed my friend Charlie Kirk. And still -- _the Left hasn't won._ But if we back down now... they will. That's why I'm asking you to stand with me -- and support an organization Charlie built to fight back and WIN: **Turning Point Action.** Charlie was assassinated for daring to teach young Americans to love this country. My father was almost murdered for refusing to give up on America. **My new book -- _Under Siege_ -- is the story of how we've endured and how YOU can help fight back.**

**For Charlie & America,** **Eric Trump**

For Charlie & America, Eric Trump On behalf of Turning Point Action P.S. The book is called " Under Siege" for a reason... Because our country is. My father almost laid down his life for America. And my friend Charlie Kirk made the ultimate sacrifice. It won't be in vain. In fact, Charlie's movement will only grow stronger. | | TURNING POINT ACTION, INC. IS A 501(C)(4) NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO PROMOTING SOCIAL WELFARE THROUGH RAISING AWARENESS ABOUT FREE MARKETS AND CAPITALISM, INITIATING CIVIC ACTION AMONGST THE YOUNGER GENERATION, AND EDUCATING YOUTH IN ORDER TO ESTABLISH FREE MARKET POLICIES INTO LAW. DONATIONS ARE NOT TAX-DEDUCTIBLE. NO GOODS OR SERVICES WERE PROVIDED IN EXCHANGE FOR YOUR CONTRIBUTION UNLESS OTHERWISE SPECIFIED.
